    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * Address PR #38 follow-up review: reliability + robustness fixes
    
    Seven distinct fixes grouped because they share the same review thread and
    touch overlapping files. Each is scoped narrowly to the concrete bug.
    
    1. Surface a 502 when every Gmail account in a multi-account search fails
       (api/plow/connectors/gmail/router.py). _search_single_account now returns
       (messages, err) so the aggregator can distinguish "no results" from
       "every account errored". Partial failure still degrades gracefully and
       returns what succeeded; the 502 fires only when there is nothing else
       to show. Masking a broken refresh token or a rate-limit as an empty
       200 OK is worse than telling the caller the request failed. Covered by
       a new test_search_returns_502_when_all_accounts_fail router test.
    
    2. Unify the settings source inside the Gmail router
       (api/plow/connectors/gmail/router.py). _require_gmail_connection now
       reads request.app.state.settings like _get_all_gmail_connections, instead
       of the module-level plow.config.settings. Test harnesses override
       app.state.settings via _make_client, so the previous import meant
       single-account code paths silently ignored test overrides. Dropped the
       module-level import since it has no other uses.
    
    3. Extend the advisory lock to every connector_tokens write path
       (api/plow/db/connectors.py). Extracted _lock_connector_row helper and
       called it from upsert_connector_token, delete_connector_token, and
       set_default_connector_token. Previously only upsert held the lock, so
       two concurrent set-default calls (or a delete that triggered
       _promote_default_if_needed racing with an upsert) could both clear and
       re-assign is_default and violate connector_tokens_one_default_idx. The
       partial unique index would catch the collision, but the loser would
       surface as a 500 instead of serializing cleanly.
    
    4. Drop the _EmptyBody sentinel from the Gmail CLI
       (cli/plow/commands/gmail.py). api_post already accepts BaseModel | None
       and emits "{}" when payload is None. The CLI was instantiating an
       _EmptyBody for the disconnect and set-default calls, which served no
       purpose beyond satisfying the old non-optional signature. Passing None
       directly removes dead code and the pydantic import.
    
    5. Fall back to stripped HTML when quoting a reply or forward whose
       original has no text/plain part (api/plow/connectors/gmail/connector.py).
       Added a small HTMLParser-based _HTMLTextExtractor and _quotable_body
       helper that prefers text_body and degrades to HTML. Before this, users
       replying to an HTML-only newsletter got an empty quoted block with no
       explanation. Uses stdlib html.parser — no new dependency.
    
    6. Replace startswith-based subject prefix checks with regexes
       (api/plow/connectors/gmail/connector.py). _RE_SUBJECT_PREFIX matches
       re/aw/antw with optional [n] or (n) suffixes so replies to already
       threaded mail ("Re[2]: subject") do not become "Re: Re[2]: subject".
       _FWD_SUBJECT_PREFIX covers fw, fwd, and wg.
    
    7. Cap attachment size in _get_gmail_attachment
       (api/plow/connectors/gmail/connector.py). Added MAX_GMAIL_ATTACHMENT_BYTES
       (32 MiB — Gmail's own ceiling is 25 MiB, with headroom) and raise
       GmailConnectorError if the server-reported size is over. This also
       bounds forward_gmail_message's per-attachment fetch loop so a multi-
       attachment forward cannot balloon memory on an API worker.
    
    Tests: api test-fast = 515 passed, cli check = 47 passed, lint + basedpyright
    clean in both projects.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * feat(app): render all connected Gmail accounts in Settings row
    
    Phoenix was structurally blind to the merged backend's `accounts[]`
    array — ConnectorStatusPayload only decoded `connected` and
    `display_name`, so even when upsert_connector_token landed a second
    account via the "+ Add another" flow, the Settings row kept showing
    just the default account's email. This closes the display gap.
    
    - ActivationState.swift: new `ConnectorAccount` struct (address + default flag).
    - DaemonClient.swift: AccountInfoPayload decoder; ConnectorStatusPayload
      now decodes the optional accounts[] array; `connectorAccounts: [String: [ConnectorAccount]]`
      observed property populated by hydrateConnectorStates alongside
      connectorStates, so the @Observable machinery re-renders the row
      automatically when a new account lands.
    - SettingsView.connectorSubtitle: when 1+ accounts are present, renders
      a comma-separated list with the default first (sorted default → then
      alphabetical). Falls back to the existing single-account display path
      when the backend only returns displayName (e.g. Slack, or pre-multi
      account state). Still shows "Connecting…" / "Connection timed out"
      states on top.
    
    Verified against the live slowdown backend: user 1 now has
    so@cncorp.ai (default) + test@plow.co connected; after this patch both
    will render inline under the Gmail row.
    
    Per-account management (disconnect, set-default, per-row dots menu) is
    a deliberate follow-up — this change is read-only display of the list.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * app: variant-aware `just app test` + ruff format follow-up
    
    Two small follow-ups from running the full root `just test-fast` for the
    first time in a fresh worktree:
    
    1. `app/justfile` `test` recipe was the last Xcode-touching recipe that
       silently fell back to the non-variant `Plow Dev` / `co.plow.app.dev`
       when no `PLOW_VARIANT` was resolved, AND did not pass
       `-derivedDataPath`, so xcodebuild wrote the test host into Xcode's
       global DerivedData. Exactly the gap flagged in section 1 of
       `plans/2026-04-10-variant-aware-everywhere.md`.
    
       Repaired to mirror the variant plumbing used by `up` / `build` /
       `package`: resolve variant from an optional positional or `.env.local`,
       derive `variant_id`, `variant_name`, `effective_bundle_id`,
       `effective_app_name`, and per-variant `derived_path`, then pass
       `-derivedDataPath "$derived_path"` to xcodebuild. `variant=""` still
       works for CI / release contexts but now refuses to proceed when the
       machine has any `co.plow.app.dev.*` Application Support dirs — if
       variant data exists on the machine, running the non-variant bundle is
       almost always a worktree misconfiguration, and the error is more
       helpful than silent cross-variant corruption.
    
       Prints a one-line banner before invocation so the operator sees
       exactly which variant / bundle / derived-data path the test host is
       about to use, and `-quiet` drops the per-test chatter now that the
       banner carries the context.
    
       Verified: with `PLOW_VARIANT=pr38` in `.env.local`, the whole root
       `just test-fast` chain now runs clean in a fresh worktree and lands
       the Swift test host at
       `../.build/xcode-derived-pr38/Build/Products/Debug/Plow Dev pr38.app`
       with bundle id `co.plow.app.dev.pr38`. No touches to
       `~/Library/Application Support/co.plow.app.dev/` or to other
       worktrees' variant dirs.
    
    2. `ruff format` wanted one line in `api/plow/connectors/gmail/connector.py`
       collapsed from a multi-line `raise` into a single line. Harmless
       mechanical reflow; root `just test-fast` runs `ruff format --check`
       and would have failed without it.
    
    Root `just test-fast` on this worktree now passes end-to-end:
      api 515, cli 47, dtu-linq 22, dtu-gmail 22, dtu-google-oauth 13,
      plowd 6, Phoenix (variant-isolated) exit 0. All lint + basedpyright
      clean across every subproject.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * api: variant-aware _ensure-db{s} + test-fast (drop hardcoded 55432/55433)
    
    Before: `api/justfile` had `_test_compose := "PLOW_DB_PORT=55432
    PLOW_TEST_DB_PORT=55433 docker compose -f ../compose.yaml"`. This hardcoded
    host ports for every `just api test-fast` invocation and — more importantly —
    never set `PLOW_COMPOSE_PROJECT`, so compose fell back to its default (`plow`)
    no matter which worktree triggered it. Two worktrees couldn't run tests in
    parallel: the second would recreate the first worktree's containers and they'd
    race on the host ports.
    
    Meanwhile `compose.yaml` is already variant-aware (line 3:
    `name: ${PLOW_COMPOSE_PROJECT:-plow}`, each service's `ports:` reading
    `${PLOW_*_PORT:-...}`), and `api/justfile`'s `up` recipe already uses
    `.plow-dev-env` via `docker compose --env-file`. The test recipes just
    skipped that machinery.
    
    After:
    
    1. New `_ensure-dev-env` helper regenerates `../.plow-dev-env` from
       `PLOW_VARIANT` (or `../.env.local`) idempotently. Same pattern as `up`.
    
    2. `_ensure-db` and `_ensure-dbs` are bash-script recipes that depend on
       `_ensure-dev-env` and use
       `docker compose --env-file ../.plow-dev-env -f ../compose.yaml`. Each
       worktree's test stack lands on its own project name (`plow-pr38`,
       `plow-wt1`, …) with disjoint host ports derived from the variant slot
       hash. Parallel `just api test-fast` across multiple worktrees is now
       safe — verified locally by watching `docker ps` show both `plow-pr38-*`
       and the legacy `plow-*` containers coexisting on 18952/18953 and
       55432/55433 respectively.
    
    3. `test-fast` is now a bash-script recipe that, after `_ensure-dbs`
       brings the containers up, sources `../.plow-dev-env` and exports
       `TEST_DB_PORT` and `DB_PORT` to match the variant's test-db host port.
       `api/tests/testenv.py`'s `setdefault` honors the exports over the
       committed `.env.test-common` values, so pytest connects to the right
       container.
    
    4. Dropped the `_test_compose` constant — nothing references it anymore.
    
    CI path is untouched. `test-ci` (the recipe CI uses) does not depend on
    `_ensure-*`, and CI boots Postgres service containers directly on host
    55432/55433 before exporting DB_PORT/TEST_DB_PORT itself. `.env.test-common`
    keeps 55433 as a bare-`pytest` fallback for that flow.
    
    Verified: root `just test-fast` in the `pr38` worktree runs clean —
    api 515, cli 47, dtu-linq 22, dtu-gmail 22, dtu-google-oauth 13, plowd 6,
    Phoenix variant-isolated, all lint + basedpyright green. Pre-existing
    `plow-db-1` / `plow-test-db-1` containers left alone.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * connector: revert subject-prefix regex, keep simple startswith
    
    Reverts the regex-based Re:/Fwd: prefix detection from fix #6 of the
    PR #38 follow-up review commit. Going back to `startswith("re:")` and
    `startswith(("fwd:", "fw:"))`.
    
    Why: the regex was cosmetic polish, not correctness. Gmail threading is
    determined by In-Reply-To / References / threadId, not subject text, so
    the only thing a prefix check controls is whether the sent folder shows
    "Re: Project status" or "Re: Re[2]: Project status". The regex added a
    maintenance burden (two patterns, whack-a-mole for every localized prefix)
    for zero functional improvement, and carried a minor false-positive risk
    on subjects like `re:port:`.
    
    If someone ever complains about stacked prefixes on a localized thread,
    the fix belongs next to a real test case, not as preemptive i18n.
    
    `fw:` / `fwd:` detection is preserved — that was already a `startswith`
    tuple on the tip of the branch before fix #6.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * gmail: stop threading forwards into the original conversation
    
    forward_gmail_message was passing threadId on send, which appended the
    forward into the source thread. Native Gmail forwards break the thread
    and start a new conversation; doing otherwise surprises both senders and
    recipients (and contradicts the PR description, which already claimed
    this behavior).
    
    Drop the threadId branch from the forward send body and add a regression
    test asserting forwards never carry threadId.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * cli: drop --account requirement from gmail read/write commands
    
    reply/forward/attachment/labels/label/modify/drafts/draft-create/draft-send/
    draft-delete were going through _require_account, but search/get/send already
    default-fall-back on the server side. The inconsistency had no upside: a
    single-account user had to type --account on every reply but not on every
    send. Pick the user-friendly rule and apply it everywhere — pass
    account=args.account or None and let _require_gmail_connection resolve to
    the default.
    
    set-default still requires --account because that command targets a
    specific account by definition.
    
    Add CLI tests covering the default-fallback path for reply and labels.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * gmail: surface per-account failures via DataResponse.meta.degraded_accounts
    
    Multi-account search aggregated per-account failures, logged them, and
    returned 200 OK with a silently-truncated result set. A client looking
    at search results had no way to know one account had silently dropped
    out — "no mail" and "your other gmail token was just revoked" looked
    identical.
    
    Add an optional `meta` dict to DataResponse (existing clients ignore it)
    and populate `meta.degraded_accounts` on partial failures with the
    account identifier and stringified error per failed account. Full
    success keeps `meta` absent. The all-fail 502 path is unchanged.
    
    Cover both cases in the router test: partial failure asserts the
    degraded entry is present; full success asserts meta is None.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * db: scope list_connector_tokens by user_id to prevent IDOR
    
    list_connector_tokens(session, connector) filtered only by connector,
    returning every user's tokens for that provider. No production caller
    exercised the missing filter, but the signature invited misuse — any
    new caller could forget to scope and silently leak tokens across users.
    
    Make user_id a required positional argument and filter the query.
    Update the test to seed two users with overlapping connectors and
    assert each user only sees their own row.
    
    Refresh .secrets.baseline for line-shifts in test_connectors.py.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * app: isolate `just app test` DerivedData from `up`/`build` to stop FDA churn
    
    The `test` recipe forces `CODE_SIGN_IDENTITY=- CODE_SIGNING_REQUIRED=NO`
    and, since 6ba6e312, was writing into the same per-variant DerivedData
    (`../.build/xcode-derived-${variant_id}`) that `just app up` and `just
    app build` use. That meant a `just app test` run would overwrite the
    dev-signed `Plow Dev (variant).app` bundle with an ad-hoc-signed copy,
    flipping its cdhash and the designated requirement TCC matches against,
    which broke the FDA grant on the next launch.
    
    Point the test recipe at a sibling `…-${variant_id}-test` (or
    `xcode-derived-dev-test` for the non-variant case) so the test host
    build never touches the bundle the user actually launches and grants
    permissions to. Costs an extra DerivedData tree per variant; eliminates
    the cross-contamination.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * app: give `just app test` a distinct bundle id to stop FDA churn
    
    Follow-up to b8826826. Separating DerivedData paths was necessary but
    not sufficient: the test host and the up/build bundle still shared
    `co.plow.app.dev[.variant]`, so LaunchServices kept both registered
    and could launch either one under the same bundle id. When the test
    variant (ad-hoc signed, no designated requirement) got picked, TCC
    stored or matched its csreq against that ad-hoc cdhash, and the next
    dev-signed rebuild no longer matched — Full Disk Access silently
    reverted without any user action.
    
    Give the test target its own bundle id (`co.plow.app.dev[.variant].test`)
    and its own .app name ("Plow Dev [variant] Test") so LaunchServices
    and TCC treat them as two different apps. Drop the now-obsolete
    non-variant collision guard it was compensating for.
    
    Verified end-to-end: `just test` passes against the new naming, and
    `/plow-activate` successfully re-grants FDA against the dev-signed
    binary without the test host stealing the bundle id.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

    * plans: multi-account Gmail routing design + 402 cross-reference
    
    Spec for fixing the silent fall-through-to-default-account routing bug
    that surfaced on chat_1 msg_88–90 as "those messages may live in a
    separate mailbox the Gmail API can't directly retrieve" (real cause:
    messages.get routed to the wrong connector token because the OpenClaw
    tool plugin has no way to pass account from a search result).
    
    Design:
    - account required at every layer (API schemas, FastAPI route,
      CLI parser, OpenClaw tool schemas)
    - messages.list stays optional-account for multi-account fan-out,
      which is the mechanism the agent uses to discover which account
      a message lives in
    - PLOW_GMAIL_DEFAULT_ACCOUNT env var as a CLI-only human convenience;
      never used on the agent path
    - CLI api_post emits structured error JSON on stdout before non-zero
      exit so the tool plugin returns typed error fields to the LLM
      instead of a stringified shell error
    - TOOLS.md/AGENTS.md/USER.md updates teach the agent the routing rule
      and the "report tool errors verbatim, never fabricate" rule
    
    Also cross-references the agent-402 bug doc so the shared
    hallucination meta-bug isn't split across two unlinked plans.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * plans: multi-account Gmail routing implementation plan
    
    9 tasks, TDD red-green-commit cadence, full code blocks at every step.
    Matches the design spec at plans/2026-04-11-multi-account-gmail-routing-design.md
    with one refinement: USER.md injection is replaced by having the agent call
    plow_gmail_status on demand (simpler, no plowd code changes, no stale-file
    risk when accounts are reconnected mid-session).
    
    Task layout:
      1. API schemas: flip 11 request models from optional to required account
      2. API router: tighten _require_gmail_connection signature
      3. CLI: _resolve_account helper + PLOW_GMAIL_DEFAULT_ACCOUNT env var
      4. CLI client.py: structured JSON error on stdout
      5. OpenClaw plow-cli plugin: account required on 15 gmail tool schemas
      6. OpenClaw plow-cli plugin: parse structured errors in makeToolExecute
         + new node --test file covering schema contract and error round-trip
      7. AGENTS.md: multi-account rules + error-reporting rules,
         guarded by test_installer_view_source.py string assertions
      8. Full test run + end-to-end verification on slowdown / chat_1
      9. PR #38 body update with "Breaking: Gmail API contract" subsection
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * api(gmail): require account on single-message and send endpoints
    
    Flip GmailGetRequest, GmailSendRequest, GmailReplyRequest,
    GmailForwardRequest, GmailLabelsListRequest, GmailModifyLabelsRequest,
    GmailAttachmentGetRequest, GmailDraftsListRequest, GmailDraftCreateRequest,
    GmailDraftSendRequest, and GmailDraftDeleteRequest from
    'account: str | None = None' to 'account: str'.
    
    FastAPI now returns 422 when account is missing instead of silently
    falling through to the default connector token, which was the root
    cause of the multi-account routing bug on chat_1 where messages.get
    queried the default account (so@cncorp.ai) for IDs that belonged to
    test@plow.co and the gmail connector raised GmailConnectorError that
    _gmail_api_call wrapped as HTTP 502.
    
    GmailListRequest is deliberately unchanged — messages.list keeps the
    optional account so multi-account fan-out search continues to be the
    agent's discovery mechanism for finding which account owns a message.
    
    Six new tests assert the 422 contract on get/send/reply/forward/
    modify-labels/attachments.get. Existing tests updated to pass
    "account": "test@example.com" in their request bodies.

    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * api(gmail): drop None default on _require_gmail_connection parameter
    
    account_identifier is now required at the type level. With Task 1's
    schema change, no caller can pass None — GmailListRequest is the only
    model with an optional account, and gmail_messages_list guards its
    call to _require_gmail_connection behind 'if body.account:'. Dropping
    the default removes the remaining path by which a caller could
    accidentally route to the user's is_default token.
    
    basedpyright passes; all 77 gmail connector tests still green.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * cli(gmail): resolve account from -a or PLOW_GMAIL_DEFAULT_ACCOUNT, no silent default
    
    Replace _require_account with _resolve_account:
      1. --account / -a beats everything
      2. PLOW_GMAIL_DEFAULT_ACCOUNT env var as a human convenience
      3. Error out with a message naming both mechanisms
    
    Every non-search gmail subcommand (get, send, reply, forward, attachment,
    labels, label, archive/star/read/unread shortcuts, drafts, draft-create,
    draft-send, draft-delete) now resolves the account before building its
    request. search is exempt — its account stays optional so server-side
    fan-out still works. set-default keeps its own strict 'account is
    required' path (no env-var fallback) because 'make unknown account
    default' is nonsense. disconnect keeps its explicit ?account= query
    param.
    
    The env var is a CLI-only convenience for humans running 'plow gmail' in
    a terminal. The OpenClaw tool plugin does not set or read it, so the
    agent path (Task 5) always passes an explicit -a.
    
    Previously-passing test_reply_without_account_falls_back_to_default and
    test_labels_without_account_falls_back_to_default were documenting the
    OLD silent-fallback behavior that caused the chat_1 routing bug — they
    are replaced with test_*_without_account_exits_with_error counterparts
    that assert SystemExit(1) and a helpful stderr message.
    
    Four new positive tests cover env-var resolution, explicit-beats-env
    precedence, and search's exemption from the rule.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * cli: emit structured JSON error on stdout in api_post before exiting
    
    The OpenClaw tool plugin (Task 6) captures plow CLI stdout as the tool
    result. Previously an HTTP error turned into a shell-exit-1 with a flat
    stderr string that the plugin wrapped into 'plow exited with code 1:
    /v1/... failed (502): ...'. The LLM then paraphrased that into
    'returning 404' or 'messages live in a separate mailbox the Gmail API
    can't directly retrieve' — because the status code was buried in prose,
    the LLM had no typed field to latch onto.
    
    api_post now prints
      {\"error\": {\"path\": \"/v1/...\", \"status_code\": 502, \"detail\": \"...\"}}
    on stdout before exiting 1, and keeps the human-readable message on
    stderr. The tool plugin (Task 6) parses stdout regardless of exit code
    and hands the LLM typed fields.
    
    Two new tests cover the JSON shape (with a parseable detail body and a
    fallback where response.json() throws).
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * openclaw(plow-cli): require account on 15 gmail tool schemas
    
    Every gmail tool that targets a specific message or creates outbound
    mail now requires an 'account' parameter in its JSON schema and passes
    it as -a <account> to the CLI. plow_gmail_search and plow_gmail_status
    are intentionally exempt — search fans out across accounts and status
    reports across all of them.
    
    Tools updated (15 total):
      plow_gmail_get        plow_gmail_send       plow_gmail_reply
      plow_gmail_forward    plow_gmail_attachment plow_gmail_labels
      plow_gmail_label      plow_gmail_archive    plow_gmail_star
      plow_gmail_mark_read  plow_gmail_mark_unread plow_gmail_drafts
      plow_gmail_draft_create plow_gmail_draft_send plow_gmail_draft_delete
    
    Each tool's description was extended with explicit routing guidance:
    'pass the account field from the search result that returned this
    message_id', or 'use the default account (from plow_gmail_status) if
    the user didn't specify which account to send from.' Closes the
    'messages.get against the wrong mailbox' hallucination path surfaced
    in chat_1 msgs_88-90.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * openclaw(plow-cli): parse structured CLI errors and expose for testing
    
    runPlow no longer rejects on non-zero exit. The CLI (cli/plow/client.py
    api_post, Task 4) now emits a JSON error payload on stdout before
    exiting 1, and makeToolExecute tries JSON.parse(stdout) first. A parsed
    object with an 'error' key is returned verbatim — the LLM sees typed
    fields like error.status_code=502 and error.detail='Gmail API error:
    ...' instead of a pre-stringified 'plow exited with code 1: ...' shell
    error.
    
    The hallucinated '404' / 'different mailbox' paraphrase in chat_1
    msg_90 was possible because the status code was buried in prose; now
    it's a typed integer the LLM can read directly.
    
    Fallback paths:
      - stdout parses as JSON, no 'error' key → success payload
      - stdout parses as JSON, has 'error' key → error payload (as-is)
      - stdout doesn't parse, exit 0              → { output: stdout }
      - stdout doesn't parse, exit != 0           → { error: { kind: 'subprocess', ... } }
      - spawn-level error (ENOENT, timeout)       → { error: { kind: 'subprocess', message } }
    
    index.test.js uses node --test (built-in, zero-deps) and covers:
      - every required-account gmail tool lists 'account' in schema
      - plow_gmail_search and plow_gmail_status don't
      - makeToolExecute round-trips a structured error payload
      - makeToolExecute round-trips a success payload on exit 0
      - makeToolExecute falls back to subprocess-error on non-JSON exit != 0
    
    makeToolExecute and __setRunPlowForTests are exported for test-time
    substitution; _runPlowImpl is a module-level mutable reference that
    defaults to the spawn-based runPlowReal and can be swapped for fakes.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * openclaw(agents): teach the agent gmail multi-account + error-reporting rules
    
    AGENTS.md now has two new sections that close the behavioral half of
    the multi-account routing fix and the broader agent hallucination
    meta-bug:
    
      Gmail — multi-account rules
        - call plow_gmail_status first to discover connected accounts +
          default
        - thread 'account' from plow_gmail_search results verbatim into
          follow-up tool calls
        - pick the from-account explicitly for send/draft_create
        - never reuse a message_id across accounts — guaranteed 502
    
      Reporting tool errors
        - read error.status_code and error.detail literally, no paraphrasing
        - 402 means out of credits; tell the user, do not retry, do not
          call another LLM-backed tool in the same turn
        - no fabricated recovery narration ('maybe it lives in a different
          mailbox', 'the connector might not support this')
        - no pre-tool preamble ('forwarding it now' before the forward
          tool returns)
    
    test_installer_view_source.py now contains two string-assertion guards
    (test_agents_md_has_gmail_multi_account_routing_rules and
    test_agents_md_has_error_reporting_rules) so someone editing AGENTS.md
    can't silently delete the rules.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * tests: ruff format new CLI test + thread account through gmail scenarios

    1. cli/tests/test_gmail_command.py — ruff format pass (test_send_*

    2. scenarios/test_gmail.py — test_gmail_get_message and test_gmail_send

    /plow-activate, re-granting FDA, and reading the agent's natural-
    language response. The test infrastructure can't judge whether the
    reply is honest; only the user can. Mark verified after the live replay.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

    * feat(app): descriptive warmup status + DRY status indicator colors
    
    Replace generic "Warming up your agent" with specific status messages:
    "Downloading openclaw-vm (42%)", "Starting PlowD", "Connecting to

    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * fix(ws): force-kill half-open WebSocket on close timeout
    
    When the backend connection dies silently (TCP half-open), ws.close()
    hangs in CLOSING state waiting for a close handshake that never comes.
    The heartbeat detects the dead connection via pong timeout, but the
    socket stays stuck for minutes until the OS TCP stack gives up.
    
    Add a 5s close timeout that force-kills the socket and triggers
    reconnect if onclose doesn't fire after ws.close(). Worst-case
    detection drops from 10+ minutes to ~30 seconds.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * refactor(ui): unify status label + color into RuntimeHeaderState
    
    Expand RuntimeHeaderState from 4 daemon-only cases to the full lifecycle
    (downloading, extracting, preparingVM, starting, connecting, gatewayError,
    running, degraded, offline). Each case now carries both .label and .color,
    eliminating the separate statusLabel() on RuntimeDownloadManager and
    headerTitle in StatusView.
    
    runtimeHeaderState() takes download state alongside daemon state so
    DaemonClient.headerState is the single source of truth. DownloadBarView
    takes the state enum directly instead of 4 loose params.

    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * fix: address PR review — migration precision, CLI JSON errors, routing distinctness
    
    Five fixes flagged in PR review:
    
    1. Migration backfill: only mark the oldest token per (user_id, connector)
       as default. The old UNIQUE (user_id, connector) constraint made the
       blanket update safe in practice, but a more precise SQL form is robust
       against stray rows and documents intent.
    
    2. CLI _resolve_account errors now emit structured JSON on stdout so the
       OpenClaw plugin can read typed status_code / detail fields instead of
       scraping stderr. Without this, the hallucination fix broke down for the
       "missing --account" failure mode specifically — which is exactly where
       the agent used to invent "404" paraphrases. `exit_error` is extended
       with optional status_code and path kwargs; existing callers unchanged.
    
    3. require_connector_token now raises ConnectorAccountNotFoundError when a
       specific account_identifier was requested but not found, and the Gmail
       router maps it to HTTP 404 with a status-checking hint. Previously both
       "nothing connected" and "wrong account name" collapsed into a single
       503, making typos look like transient outages.
    
    4. GmailMessageSummary.account is now required (no `""` default). The
       connector layer stamps it from connection.email_address at construction
       time, so every response path sets it explicitly — `_with_account` is
       gone. A routing bug that skips this step now fails Pydantic validation
       at construction instead of silently emitting an empty-string field.
    
    5. Slack provider validates account_identifier at the source: if Slack
       accepts the OAuth exchange but returns neither a team nor a user id,
       the provider raises 502 with a Slack-specific message instead of
       letting the empty string reach oauth_callback's generic check.
    
    New tests:
    - api/tests/db/test_connectors.py: require_connector_token raises the
      right exception for "nothing connected" vs. "wrong account."
    - api/tests/connectors/gmail/test_api_router.py: wrong-account → 404.
    - api/schemas/tests/test_api_schemas.py: missing account on
      GmailMessageSummary fails Pydantic validation.
    - api/tests/connectors/slack/test_provider.py: OAuth response with no
      team/user id surfaces a typed 502 at the provider.
    - cli/tests/test_gmail_command.py: stdout JSON payload verified for
      reply, labels, and set-default missing-account paths.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * fix: account-qualified Gmail miss returns 503 when zero tokens; HTML-escape forward headers; DRY Account type alias
    
    - db.connectors: require_connector_token now distinguishes "user has zero
      tokens" (503, "connect Gmail") from "user has others but not this one"
      (404, "check status") when an account_identifier is supplied. The old
      code raised ConnectorAccountNotFoundError unconditionally, sending
      first-time users on a "wrong account name" recovery path with no exit.
      One extra DB query on the error path only; happy path unchanged.
    - tests/db/test_connectors: add regression test for zero-tokens +
      account-specified raising ConnectorNotConnectedError.
    - schemas/api/gmail: introduce Account = Annotated[str, Field(min_length=1)]
      type alias and apply to all 13 account fields. Empty-string account is
      now rejected at the API boundary (422) instead of degrading to a 404
      from the repository lookup. Drops the 5-line rationale comment on
      GmailMessageSummary.account since the alias centralizes the invariant.
    - connectors/gmail/connector: html.escape() the four interpolated Gmail
      headers (From/Date/Subject/To) and the user's note body in
      forward_gmail_message's HTML branch. Closes the XSS-adjacent surface
      where a crafted external header could land verbatim in forwarded HTML.
    - connectors/gmail/router: tighten the fan-out gate to `body.account is
      not None` now that empty strings are rejected at validation.

    * refactor(gmail): split connection types, cache OAuth tokens, add Depends factory
    
    - `GmailConnection` now carries a freshly-validated `access_token` and a
      required `email_address`. The pre-profile-fetch state moves to a new
      `GmailUnboundConnection` so the type system enforces "every connector
      call has an owning mailbox" instead of running asserts at runtime.
    
    - `token_service.get_gmail_connection` reads `token.access_token` /
      `token.token_expiry` and reuses the cached value when it's still valid;
      otherwise it refreshes against Google and persists. The connector
      module no longer refreshes per call — every call gets a ready-to-use
      token, eliminating one OAuth round-trip per Gmail API request and
      ~8x for multi-account fanout.
    
    - New `require_gmail(...)` Depends factory replaces per-endpoint
      `_require_gmail_connection(...)` boilerplate. Endpoints now declare
      their needs with `Depends(require_gmail("messages.get"))` and call
      `await gmail(body.account)` to resolve. The shared
      `_require_gmail_connection` helper remains as a single seam for tests.
    
    - Aggregate attachment cap (`MAX_GMAIL_FORWARD_TOTAL_BYTES = 50 MB`) on
      `forward_gmail_message`, plus a `_post_raw_message` helper that DRYs
      send/reply/forward/draft-create's identical send-tail.

    * refactor: DRY OpenClaw plugin, CLI dispatch, parametrize tests, share b64url_pad

      single `makeGmailTool` builder + `asList`/`pushList` helpers. Plugin
      drops from 1030 → 883 lines (-147) without changing the tool surface.
    - CLI gmail dispatch: 13 of 17 subcommand branches collapse into a `_DISPATCH`
      table keyed on subcommand name; the `_resolve_account` helper reads
      `args.gmail_command` directly so call sites stop passing the label.
    - Router validation tests: 9 nearly-identical missing-account / empty-to
      tests collapse into 2 parametrized `subTest` loops covering the same
      endpoints.
    - Shared `b64url_pad(data)` in `plow_schemas.api.gmail` replaces the
      `+ "=" * (-len(data) % 4)` formula in 5 call sites across api/cli/tests.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * refactor: rename account_identifier → account; drop derived Swift state
    
    The connector_tokens DB column is named `account_identifier` for migration
    history reasons. The Python attribute, function kwargs, and request bodies
    already use `account` everywhere they touch users — this commit aligns the
    remaining seams (model attribute, repository, OAuth provider results, raw
    helper kwargs) so the DB-column suffix stops leaking into router/Swift code.
    
    The model uses `mapped_column("account_identifier", String)` so the DB
    column name is preserved (no migration). A new `test_models.py` case
    asserts the mapping in both directions to lock the contract.
    
    Also drops the `agentReady`/`warmupStatus` derived state in `SettingsView`
    that the recent `RuntimeHeaderState` unification made redundant. `agentReady`
    now reads `client.headerState == .running` directly, which is the canonical
    "VM ready and connected and not degraded" signal.

    Onboarding bugfixes: OAuth, model ID, clock skew, .env path, menu bar, port persistence (#183)
    
    * feat: add file access separation of powers — omniscient search + sandbox isolation
    
    Plow (host) searches the full filesystem via Spotlight and recommends files
    to the user via iMessage approval links on plow.co. Approved files are copied
    into ~/Plow where OpenClaw can act on them. OpenClaw never sees anything
    outside its sandbox — it expresses intent via `plow file-request`, and Plow
    handles discovery, recommendation, and approval.

    The auto-recovery in fetchStatus() was too aggressive during initial
    startup — a transient non-ok status while the daemon was still

Author: Sam Odio <sam@odio.email>

commit ff3b2a22f326057c16d451599b45d22d1b9fbb41
Author: Daniel Delattre <55804946+delattre1@users.noreply.github.com>
Date:   Wed Apr 8 16:23:14 2026 -0300

    Add OpenClaw Marketplace — browse and install skills from ClawHub (#165)
    
    * Add npm + clawhub CLI to VM image for marketplace skill management
    
    The OpenClaw VM runtime stage was missing npm, which prevented installing
    the ClawHub CLI or any npm-based plugins. This adds npm to the Alpine
    package list and bakes clawhub globally so agents can search, install,
    and sync skills from clawhub.com out of the box.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * Add marketplace UI for browsing and installing ClawHub skills
    
    Marketplace server (FastAPI) and single-page frontend (Tailwind) that lets
    users browse, search, install, and uninstall ClawHub skills from a browser.
    
    Architecture:
    - Host-side installs: fetch zip from ClawHub API, extract to plowd-managed
      workspace skills/ dir. No VM access or SSH needed — virtiofs makes skills
      instantly visible to the agent.
    - Atomic install: extract to staging dir, validate SKILL.md exists, then
      swap. Crash-safe cleanup on failure.
    - XSS hardened: zero inline JS handlers, all interactions via data
      attributes + event delegation, DOM-based escaping, allowlisted avatar URLs.
    - ClawHub API error handling: timeout, connection, and JSON parse errors
      produce clear HTTP 502/504 responses.
    
    Frontend features:
    - Real-time search with ClawHub vector search API
    - Category filter pills (Weather, DevOps, Productivity, etc.)
    - Skill detail slide-out panel with stats, changelog, author info
    - "Try it now" section with example prompts extracted from SKILL.md
    - "Open DTU to test" button linking to localhost:18805
    - Installed tab with badge count
    
    Also updates AGENTS.md template to instruct the agent to scan
    workspace/skills/ for additional installed skills, fixing the
    discoverability gap where marketplace-installed skills were functional
    but not listed by the agent.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * Fix 5 review issues: zip bombs, atomic install, path resolution, error handling, idempotent uninstall
    
    1. Zip bomb protection: cap compressed (50MB), uncompressed (200MB),
       entry count (500), per-file size (50MB). Validate all limits before
       extracting.
    
    2. Crash-safe atomic install: rename old → .backup, rename staging →
       target, THEN delete backup. If crash between steps, backup is
       restored on next attempt.
    
    3. Resolve workspace path from PLOW_BUNDLE_ID / PLOW_LAUNCH_MODE env
       vars instead of hardcoding co.plow.app.dev. Supports dev and
       production bundles.
    
    4. Catch all httpx.HTTPError subclasses (not just Timeout/Connect).
       Sanitize error messages — never leak local paths or stack traces
       to the client.
    
    5. Idempotent uninstall: return 200 with already_removed=true instead
       of 404 when skill is already gone.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * Fix R2 review: streaming download, plowd config resolution, safe backup retention
    
    1. Streaming zip download: use httpx stream() + aiter_bytes() to
       enforce the 50MB size cap incrementally without buffering the
       entire response into memory. Each 64KB chunk is checked against
       the limit.
    
    2. Workspace path from plowd config: read PLOWD_APP_SUPPORT_ROOT
       (the canonical env var from app/plowd/plowd/config.py line 33),
       fall back to PLOW_APP_SUPPORT_ROOT (set by justfile/Mac app).
       Fail with a clear RuntimeError if neither is set — no more
       guessing bundle IDs.
    
    3. Safe backup retention: the finally block only deletes .backup-*
       if the target dir is already in place. If target is missing
       (swap failed AND restore failed), backup is preserved for manual
       recovery with a warning log.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * Fix: defer old backup deletion until new install succeeds
    
    A previous failed install may leave .backup-<slug> as the user's only
    recoverable copy. The prior code deleted it eagerly at the start of the
    next install attempt — if that attempt also failed, the skill was lost.
    
    Now: old .backup is only removed after the new staging dir is fully
    validated and ready for swap. The deletion sequence is:
    1. Extract + validate new skill in staging dir
    2. Remove old .backup (safe: we have both live target + new staging)
    3. Rename target → .backup
    4. Rename staging → target
    5. Delete .backup (new version confirmed in place)
    
    If any step fails, the .backup is preserved for manual recovery.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

    Addresses 5 CEO feedback items on the V3 (sidebar + grid) variation:
    
    1. Pagination: infinite scroll via IntersectionObserver loads 100 skills
       per page using ClawHub's offset parameter. No "Load More" button.
       Server search proxy now accepts offset parameter.
    
    2. Categories expanded: 25+ categories organized by section (Development,
       Productivity, Communication, Media & Data, Other) derived from actual
       ClawHub skill data — each category is a vector search query.
    
    3. Removed "Try it" DTU link: no more localhost:18805 references or
       hardcoded example prompts. Only ClawHub API data is shown.
    
    4. Detail panel enriched: now shows all API-provided data — author with
       avatar, active installs, comments count, created/updated dates,
       version history, changelog, slug, and "View on ClawHub" link.
    
    5. No fake data: removed skill-examples endpoint usage, removed emoji
       icons, removed hardcoded prompts. Every field in the UI comes from
       the ClawHub API response.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * Remove skill count from search placeholder
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * Add download and star counts to skill cards
    
    Cards now show download count and star count below the skill name,
    fetched asynchronously via a new POST /api/batch-stats endpoint that
    queries ClawHub's /api/skill detail for up to 20 slugs in parallel.
    
    Cards render immediately with "loading..." placeholder, then stats
    fill in as the batch response arrives. Stats are cached client-side
    so re-renders are instant.
    
    The batch endpoint uses asyncio.gather for concurrent fetches with
    a 10s per-skill timeout. Max 20 slugs per request.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

       remains. Served at root localhost:3000/ instead of /v3. Removed the
       /v{num} route from the server.

       downloads, active installs, all-time installs, stars, versions,
       comments, slug, created/updated dates, version + changelog, author
       with link to their ClawHub profile, and "View on ClawHub" link.
       No fake or made-up data — every field comes from /api/skill.

    - Malicious skills have install button replaced with "Blocked" message
    
    Server-side malicious block:
    - POST /api/install checks moderation.verdict before downloading
    - Returns HTTP 403 for malicious skills
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * Fix security scan label: 'No flags reported' instead of 'No issues detected'
    
    The ClawHub HTTP API returns moderation:null for skills where version-level
    scan data (staticScan, vtAnalysis) exists but skill-level moderationFlags
    haven't been set. The website shows version-level scans via Convex real-time
    queries that aren't exposed through the public HTTP API.
    
    Changed the null-moderation label from "No issues detected" (implies we
    scanned it) to "No flags reported" (accurately reflects what the API told us).
    Uses muted styling instead of green to avoid false confidence.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * Add sort dropdown: most downloaded, most stars, newest, recently updated
    
    ClawHub's search API doesn't support server-side sorting (results are
    always by vector similarity score, and the /api/v1/skills listing
    endpoint returns empty). Sort is done client-side using statsCache
    data from the batch-stats endpoint.
    
    Options: Relevance (default, server score), Most downloaded, Most stars,
    Newest, Recently updated. When stats-based sort is selected before stats
    finish loading, the grid re-sorts automatically once stats arrive.
    
    Sort dropdown is hidden in the Installed view since those are local-only.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * Revert client-side sort — ClawHub API does not support server-side sorting
    
    Tested: search API ignores sort/order params entirely (identical results
    with sort=downloads, sort=stars, order=desc). The /api/v1/skills listing
    endpoint which supports sort returns empty (likely needs auth).
    
    Client-side sorting on a paginated subset is wrong — it only reorders
    the current page, not the full catalog. Removed the sort dropdown and
    all sortAndRender logic.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

    db, test-db, api, dtu-linq, vm-shell, dtu-gmail, dtu-google-oauth).

    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * Fix plowd gateway startup: retry on failure, increase API timeout
    
    Root cause: fetch_model_list had a 5s timeout but the Plow API can
    take 10-15s on cold start. After 3 failed attempts, plowd gave up
    permanently — gateway marked as failed, installer stuck on
    "downloading" screen.
    
    Fixes:
    1. Increase API request timeout from 5s to 15s in manifest_client.py
       (covers model list, owner channels, tools manifest)
    
    2. Increase model-list retries from 3 to 6 with capped backoff
       (2s, 4s, 6s, 8s, 10s, 10s)
    
    3. Add gateway boot retry loop (3 attempts with 5s/10s delays).
       On failure, re-creates GatewayManager for a clean retry instead
       of giving up permanently. Only sets gateway_start_error after
       all boot attempts are exhausted.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * Fix marketplace API paths: all fetch calls must use /marketplace/ prefix
    
    The sed replacement when folding into plowd only caught single-quoted
    fetch('/api/...) calls but missed backtick template literals
    fetch(\`/api/skill/\${slug}\`). This caused skill detail, search, and
    post-install/uninstall refreshes to hit /api/ (which doesn't exist on
    plowd) instead of /marketplace/api/.
    
    Fixed all remaining bare /api/ paths to /marketplace/api/.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * Fix AGENTS.md workspace paths: use /workspace (VM guest path)
    
    Root cause: GatewayConfig.default_workspace_dir returned the host-side
    macOS path (/Users/.../Application Support/.../workspace) which was
    substituted into AGENTS.md template. The agent runs inside the VM where
    the workspace is mounted at /workspace via virtiofs — it can't access
    host paths.
    
    ALL skill references in AGENTS.md were wrong:
    - plow-file-discovery → /Users/.../skills/plow-file-discovery/SKILL.md
    - browser-usage → /Users/.../skills/browser-usage/SKILL.md
    - Additional skills scan → /Users/.../skills/
    
    Fix: default_workspace_dir now always returns /workspace since the VM
    always mounts the host workspace there via virtiofs. This fixes the
    AGENTS.md template substitution and the openclaw.json workspace config.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * Fix marketplace workspace path: use host_workspace_dir for file operations
    
    The marketplace router used settings.openclaw_workspace_dir which is
    /workspace (the VM guest path). File operations need the host-side path
    (settings.openclaw_host_workspace_dir) since the marketplace runs on the
    host, not inside the VM. The VM sees changes via virtiofs.

commit 7f6e540a4a9b46758d4e0829da5eedc2f51e27aa
Author: swagatpatel <swagatpatel@users.noreply.github.com>
Date:   Wed Apr 8 09:05:52 2026 -0700

    Fix audit log: parse ISO 8601 timestamps with fractional seconds (#163)
    
    * fix: parse ISO 8601 timestamps with fractional seconds in activity log
    
    The daemon (plowd) emits timestamps via Python's datetime.isoformat()
    which includes microseconds (e.g. 2026-04-07T22:55:52.348123+00:00).
    The default ISO8601DateFormatter in Swift cannot parse fractional seconds,
    causing all activity log entries to fall back to "just now".
    
    Add a parseISO8601 helper that tries fractional-seconds format first,
    then falls back to the standard format for compatibility.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Paperclip <noreply@paperclip.ing>
    
    * test: add XCTest unit tests for ISO 8601 timestamp parsing fix
    
    Tests cover ApprovalRequest.parseISO8601 with fractional seconds (Python
    daemon format), standard ISO 8601, Z suffix, empty/invalid strings, plus
    AgeDescription and TimestampDescription fallback behavior. All 49 tests pass.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Paperclip <noreply@paperclip.ing>
    
    * test: expand ISO 8601 timestamp test coverage for edge cases
    
    Added 7 new test cases across 3 new scenarios:
    - ISO8601ParsingTests: fractional seconds with Z suffix, milliseconds
      (3-digit), negative timezone offset, and sub-second precision
      preservation
    - ResolutionDescriptionTests: full coverage for
      ApprovalHistoryEntry.resolutionDescription (fractional, standard,
      invalid, empty) — previously untested
    
    Co-Authored-By: Paperclip <noreply@paperclip.ing>
    
    ---------
    
    Co-authored-by: Paperclip <noreply@paperclip.ing>

    Native OpenAI OAuth — no Codex CLI dependency (#151)
    
    * Preserve OpenClaw auth-profiles.json for user OAuth
    
    PlowD was deleting gateway/agents/main/agent/auth-profiles.json on
    every gateway start ("migrated-to-proxy"). This blocked users from
    authenticating directly with LLM providers via OpenClaw's built-in
    OAuth flows (e.g. openclaw models auth login --provider openai-codex).
    
    Now auth-profiles.json is preserved across restarts. The Plow proxy
    provider remains as fallback — users without OAuth still use credits.
    
    Plan: plans/2026-04-03-expose-openclaw-oauth.md

    Three new endpoints to expose OpenClaw's built-in provider OAuth:
    
    - POST /internal/openclaw/models/auth/login
      Triggers OpenClaw auth for a provider. OpenAI Codex runs browser
      OAuth; Anthropic accepts a setup-token paste.
    
    - GET /internal/openclaw/models/auth/status
      Returns which providers have valid auth profiles (plow, openai-codex,
      anthropic).
    
    - POST /internal/openclaw/model/set
      Sets agents.defaults.model.primary in openclaw.json. Switches the
      active provider/model (e.g. "openai-codex/gpt-5.4" vs "plow/claude-sonnet-4-6").

    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * Fix Anthropic direct bypass: prefix plow model IDs, add models allowlist
    
    Two root causes for Anthropic not bypassing the proxy:
    
    1. Plow provider registered models as 'anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6' —
       same ID as the native Anthropic provider. OpenClaw picked the plow
       provider. Fix: prefix with 'plow/' so plow models are

       for the user's direct auth.

       the model to agents.defaults.models (the allowlist). OpenClaw ignores
       models not in the allowlist. Fix: add the model to the allowlist.

    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * Fix Anthropic auth profile: use provider 'anthropic' not 'anthropic-setup-token'
    
    OpenClaw's listProfilesForProvider normalizes provider IDs. When
    looking for profiles for the 'anthropic' provider, it doesn't match
    'anthropic-setup-token'. The profile must use provider: 'anthropic'.

    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * Restart runtime after model switch so OpenClaw picks up new config
    
    OpenClaw reads model config at agent session start and caches it.
    Writing to openclaw.json alone doesn't change the running session.
    Now setActiveModel triggers restartRuntime() after updating config
    so the new model takes effect immediately.
    
    Also fixes Anthropic provider field (anthropic, not anthropic-setup-token).
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * Fix Codex OAuth import: extract JWT expiry instead of setting expires=0
    
    OpenClaw rejects auth profiles with expires <= 0 as 'invalid_expires'.
    Now extracts the 'exp' claim from the JWT access token and converts
    to milliseconds. Tokens from ~/.codex/auth.json are valid until their
    actual JWT expiry (e.g. 10 days from last refresh).
    
    Verified: all 3 providers working with definitive proof:
    - Anthropic direct: zero proxy calls, real token
    - OpenAI Codex: zero proxy calls, agent confirms gpt-5.4
    - Plow credits: goes through proxy as expected
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * Fix Codex import: use 30-day expiry when refresh token exists
    
    Codex CLI works with stale access tokens because it refreshes via
    refresh_token. Don't derive expires from the JWT access_token exp
    when a refresh token is present — set 30 days from now instead.
    OpenClaw's own refresh logic handles actual token refresh.
    
    New test: stale access token + valid refresh token → profile not expired.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * Remove restartRuntime from model switch — config picked up on next message
    
    OpenClaw's hybrid reload mode + per-message session creation means
    the model config is read fresh for each inbound message. No restart
    needed. The previous restartRuntime() call caused a race condition
    where PlowD was killed mid-startup, leaving the app stuck on
    "Booting up Plow".

    * Fix model switch: sync Swift UI from /status, restart gateway on change
    
    Two bugs fixed:
    
    1. Menu bar showed "Plow Credits" after switching model via API because
       DaemonClient.activeModel was never read back from PlowD. Now /status
       returns active_model from openclaw.json, and fetchStatus() syncs it
       on every poll cycle.
    
    2. Model switch required full app restart because OpenClaw caches
       agents.defaults.model.primary at gateway boot — hybrid config reload
       doesn't cover model changes. Now model/set SSHes into the VM and
       restarts just the OpenClaw gateway process (pkill + relaunch).
       The operator auto-reconnects via its existing backoff loop.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * Use SIGUSR1 for gateway restart instead of pkill+relaunch
    
    OpenClaw's built-in SIGUSR1 handler triggers an in-process restart
    that re-reads all config including model.primary. This replaces the
    previous pkill + openclaw-start.sh approach with a single signal:
    
      kill -USR1 $(pgrep -f 'openclaw gateway')
    
    Benefits:
    - No process teardown/relaunch cycle
    - Less downtime (~1-2s vs ~5-10s)
    - No risk of init script exiting if OpenClaw process dies
    - Uses OpenClaw's documented restart mechanism (commands.restart)
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * Fix SIGUSR1 pgrep pattern: match openclaw-gateway process name
    
    The OpenClaw gateway child process is named "openclaw-gateway" (hyphenated),
    not "openclaw gateway" (space-separated). The previous pgrep -f pattern
    didn't match, causing the SSH command to fail silently.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * Bypass credit gate for direct provider mode
    
    * Update active agent model on model switch
    
    * Add native OpenAI OAuth endpoint — no Codex CLI dependency
    
    New endpoints:
    - POST /internal/openclaw/models/auth/oauth — starts OAuth flow
    - GET /internal/openclaw/models/auth/oauth/status — polls completion
    
    Flow:
    1. PlowD SSHes into VM, runs `openclaw models auth login --provider
       openai-codex` with BROWSER=none (headless/paste-back mode)
    2. Captures OAuth URL from stdout (https://auth.openai.com/...)
    3. Returns URL to caller (Swift opens in Mac browser)
    4. Starts temp HTTP server on localhost:1455 for callback
    5. Browser redirects to localhost:1455/auth/callback?code=...
    6. PlowD captures redirect URL, feeds it back to OpenClaw stdin
    7. OpenClaw exchanges code for tokens, saves auth-profiles.json
    
    No port forwarding needed. Uses OpenClaw's built-in remote/headless
    paste-back mode — the callback runs on the Mac host, not in the VM.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * Wire native OAuth into Swift UI: Connect OpenAI button + browser flow
    
    Replace "Import from Codex CLI" popover with native OAuth:
    - Button: "Connect OpenAI" (was "Import from Codex CLI")
    - On click: calls POST /auth/oauth, opens returned URL in browser
    - Polls GET /auth/oauth/status every 1s until completed
    - On success: refreshes provider status, auto-selects codex model
    
    Also fixes popover text truncation:
    - .fixedSize(horizontal: false, vertical: true) for text wrapping
    - .frame(width: 280) for consistent popover width
    
    DaemonClient.startOpenAIOAuth() handles the full lifecycle:
    1. POST /auth/oauth → get URL
    2. NSWorkspace.shared.open(url) → browser
    3. Poll status until completed/error/timeout (120s)
    4. fetchProviderAuthStatus() on success
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * Fix OAuth SSH: use python3 pty.spawn + full openclaw path
    
    OpenClaw auth login requires an interactive TTY. The VM's Alpine
    busybox shell doesn't have `script`, so we use python3 pty.spawn
    to provide a PTY. Also:
    - Use full path /usr/local/bin/openclaw (not in SSH PATH)
    - Set COLUMNS=200 to prevent clack prompt narrow-terminal wrapping
    - Set BROWSER=none for headless/paste-back mode
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * Fix OAuth callback: capture event loop before spawning server thread
    
    The HTTP callback handler runs in a daemon thread. Using
    asyncio.get_event_loop() from that thread returns the wrong loop.
    Fixed by capturing loop = asyncio.get_running_loop() before starting
    the thread, then using loop.call_soon_threadsafe() directly.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

    Three fixes for the native OAuth flow:
    
    1. Set OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/config/gateway so the auth CLI writes
       auth-profiles.json to the virtiofs mount (not ~/.openclaw/ in VM)
    
    2. Use \r\n for paste-back (PTY expects carriage return to submit)
    
    3. Increase token exchange timeout from 30s to 60s

    * Validate OAuth callback: require /auth/callback path + code parameter
    
    The localhost:1455 callback server previously accepted any GET request.
    Now validates:
    - Path must be exactly /auth/callback
    - Query must contain a 'code' parameter
    
    Any other request gets 400 "Invalid callback" and does NOT resolve
    the future — the flow stays in awaiting_browser state.

    Fix MenuBarExtra ScrollView collapsing to near-zero height (#101)

    collapse on some machines. Measure the scroll content via a
    PreferenceKey and set an explicit frame height, capped at the
    screen's visible height.

    leaving isRecording=true with no audio sources. On stop, mixDown would

    mode and only degrades gracefully in mic+system mode.

    * refactor: URL-based service config, split host vs VM API URLs
    
    Runtime contract is now URL-based, not port-based:
    - Launcher passes PLOW_PLOWD_URL, PLOW_GATEWAY_URL, PLOW_API_URL
    - Swift reads full URLs via ServiceURLs (renamed from PortSlot)
    - VMManager parses ports from URLs at the edge for bridge setup
    
    Explicit host/VM API URL split:
    - PLOW_API_URL: host-side API (http://127.0.0.1:{slot-derived port})
    - PLOW_OPENCLAW_API_URL: VM-facing API (http://127.0.0.1:8000, the
      guest-local bridged endpoint via Bridge E)
    - DaemonClient.resolveOpenClawAPIURL() passes the VM-facing URL to
      plowd, which writes it to /config/gateway/plow-api-url
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * refactor: ports for plowd/gateway, URLs for API, split host vs VM API
    
    Config contract:
    - PLOW_PLOWD_PORT, PLOW_GATEWAY_PORT: ports (always loopback)
    - PLOW_API_URL: host-side API URL (app + plowd host fetches)
    - PLOW_VM_API_URL: VM-facing API URL (guest-local bridged endpoint)
    
    plowd receives both:
    - PLOWD_PLOW_API_URL: host-side, for manifest/model fetches
    - PLOWD_VM_API_URL: written to /config/gateway/plow-api-url for VM
    
    Fixes the bug where dev plowd was getting the VM-facing URL
    (127.0.0.1:8000) for its own host-side fetches instead of the
    compose API port.

    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * refactor: collapse PLOWD_PLOW_API_URL/PLOWD_VM_PLOW_API_URL into shared PLOW_*
    
    Shared runtime env vars use PLOW_* prefix (not PLOWD_*):
    - PLOW_API_URL: host-side API URL (read by app + plowd)
    - PLOW_VM_PLOW_API_URL: VM-facing API URL (read by plowd for VM config)
    
    PLOWD_* prefix reserved for daemon-private config only:
    - PLOWD_APP_SUPPORT_ROOT, PLOWD_OPENCLAW_*, etc.
    
    plowd config.py uses pydantic validation_alias to read PLOW_API_URL
    and PLOW_VM_PLOW_API_URL directly (bypassing PLOWD_ prefix).
    
    DaemonClient forwards the shared PLOW_* names to plowd instead of
    minting PLOWD_ copies. Docs updated to match.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * fix: packaged plowd launch path uses shared PLOW_* env names
    
    The packaged (prod) launch path still used PLOWD_PLOW_API_URL and
    PLOWD_VM_PLOW_API_URL. Updated to forward PLOW_API_URL and
    PLOW_VM_PLOW_API_URL, matching the dev path and the config.py
    validation_alias. Also updates .env.example to document the
    shared env var names.

    zsh -cu (nounset) requires ${var:-} guards for potentially empty
    variables. Fixes `just app up` crash with "parameter not set".

    * refactor: separate backend and app lifecycles
    
    Backend (compose) and app are now independent:
    - `just api up`: starts full compose stack (db + api + dtu) in
      foreground. Logs visible. Ctrl-C stops it. Run in its own tab.
    - `just app up`: builds and launches macOS app only. No compose
      lifecycle. Kill and relaunch freely without restarting backend.
    - `just api down`: tears down compose stack.
    
    Compose runs in foreground (not -d) so API/DTU logs are visible
    in the terminal. The app cleanup no longer tears down compose.

commit ad4e121fc43e8f99402f48a7bdace8623b498e24
Author: Swagat Patel <swagatpatel@users.noreply.github.com>
Date:   Thu Apr 30 15:09:05 2026 -0700

    fix(phoenix): include DownloadBarView height in installer auto-size
    
    The bottom-anchored DownloadBarView is a ZStack overlay, so SettingsView's
    ScrollView didn't know about it; when settings content fit, the last rows
    sat hidden behind the bar (worse in the retry state, where the bar grows).
    
    Add a second preference key (InstallerOverlayHeightKey) that reports the
    bar's measured height to InstallerView. targetWindowHeight now adds it to
    the connectors-screen target so the bar always sits below the content.
    The key reverts to 0 when the bar leaves the view tree (post-install),
    restoring the bar-less sizing.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit c06d059ebde64258a8c22cc40f38b7730d22e2a3
Author: Swagat Patel <swagatpatel@users.noreply.github.com>
Date:   Thu Apr 30 14:55:29 2026 -0700

    fix(phoenix): auto-size installer window to settings content
    
    Replace the hard-coded 530/610 window heights with a SwiftUI preference
    key (InstallerContentHeightKey) that propagates SettingsView's measured
    content height up to InstallerView. The hosting NSWindow resizes to fit
    that height, capped by visibleFrame - 80 so the existing ScrollView
    still acts as a fallback when content genuinely exceeds the screen.
    
    Splash screens (welcomeInstall, FDA, activation, connectContext) keep
    the design-set 530 since their layouts rely on Spacer fill, not
    intrinsic content height.

commit f60cdd31d430065d40cbd64a7546ab7e0da8b951
Author: swagatpatel <swagatpatel@users.noreply.github.com>
Date:   Wed Apr 29 11:18:36 2026 -0700

    feat: 1Password broker — host-side op integration for agent credential retrieval (#538)
    
    Host-side broker that lets an openclaw agent retrieve a user's 1Password credentials via the `op` CLI on the Mac host. No service account; uses the desktop app's biometric integration. Spike-quality — proves the architecture and surfaces productization gaps.
    
    ## What ships
    
    - Plowd broker module (`app/plowd/plowd/onepassword.py`) + three internal HTTP endpoints (`/internal/op/{whoami,list-logins,fetch-credential}`), bearer-auth gated.
    - `plow op-test` CLI for host-side broker smoke testing.
    - Openclaw plugin (`app/openclaw/plugins/onepassword/`) registering `onepassword_list_logins` and `onepassword_fetch_credential` agent tools.
    - SKILL.md teaching the agent the index → match → fetch pattern.
    - VM/Lima/Phoenix wiring to mount the new plugin alongside `plow-cli`.
    - CI: `just test-fast` / `just test-ci` now run `app/openclaw/plugins/*/index.test.js` via a new `test-openclaw-plugins` recipe (also covers previously-orphaned `plow-cli` and `guard` tests).
    
    ## Validation
    
    End-to-end: agent successfully filled login forms on `practicetestautomation.com` and on a real Amazon account using credentials from a real 1Password Personal vault. Plowd log audit confirmed zero credential leakage.
    
    ## Architectural note
    
    The spike's design exposes the credential index AND plaintext to the LLM context. 1Password's official [Agentic Autofill](https://developer.1password.com/docs/agentic-autofill/) explicitly rejects this shape. See [`docs/architecture/2026-04-27-onepassword-broker-spike-findings.md`](docs/architecture/2026-04-27-onepassword-broker-spike-findings.md) for paths forward — Path B (server-side URL match) is the recommended next architectural step.
    
    ## Out of scope (productize later)
    
    TOTP / non-Login categories, multi-account selection UX, vault filter, multi-candidate disambiguation, bundled `op` binary, index TTL/persistence. Encryption-at-rest is N/A — broker stores nothing.

 app/Phoenix/DaemonClient.swift  | 1 +
 app/Phoenix/LimaVMManager.swift | 2 ++
 2 files changed, 3 insertions(+)

commit e247b5bf40b389a3e963e02c79e5c2b7351f3a4a
Author: Sam Odio <sam@odio.email>
Date:   Tue Apr 28 07:10:25 2026 -0700

    skills: prefer Plow team signing in plow-{dev,prod}-install, ad-hoc fallback (#521)
    
    * skills: prefer Plow team signing in plow-{dev,prod}-install, ad-hoc fallback
    
    Both skills used to assume a single signing path:
    - /plow-dev-install relied on Xcode auto-signing with the cncorp team
      (KLP7XF8JXJ), which broke as soon as a new dev machine had only the
      Plow Collective team (3559PD337Z) cert in the keychain.
    - /plow-prod-install hardcoded ad-hoc Release signing, which means TCC
      grants reset on every rebuild even when a Plow Developer ID
      Application cert is available.
    
    Both now do the same thing: detect whether an Apple Development
    (/dev/) or Developer ID Application (/prod/) cert exists for the Plow
    team, fall back to cncorp during the migration, then to ad-hoc if
    neither is in the keychain. Dev writes Local.xcconfig; prod sets
    PLOW_CODE_SIGN_IDENTITY for the build recipe to honor.
    
    Build recipe: app/Justfile's Release ad-hoc routing was unconditional
    — it overwrote PLOW_CODE_SIGN_IDENTITY="-" and re-signed ad-hoc even
    when the caller had set a real cert. Both branches are now guarded so
    a caller-supplied identity wins.
    
    Local.xcconfig.example: documents both team IDs and notes that
    /plow-dev-install auto-manages the file on each run.
    
    Also documents two fresh-Mac gotchas hit while bringing up a new dev
    box:
    - WWDR G3 must be in the keychain with "Use System Defaults" trust,
      not "Always Trust" (the GUI's "Always Trust" sets TrustAsRoot on the
      intermediate, breaking codesign chain validation with
      errSecInternalComponent).
    - Local Network permission must be granted for Tailscale-backed dev
      hosts on Tahoe; per-variant bundle ids re-prompt for each new
      worktree.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * review fixes for PR #521 — fix team-signed seal, dev fallback, lowercase justfile
    
    Address review feedback:
    
    - app/justfile: post-package re-sign now runs for any non-empty
      PLOW_CODE_SIGN_IDENTITY, not just `-`. plowd-package-runtime rsyncs
      into Contents/Resources/runtime AFTER Xcode signs, so without the
      re-sign team-signed bundles ship with an invalid seal (knightwatch #1,
      blocking).
    - plow-prod-install Step 4: add `codesign --verify --deep --strict` so a
      broken seal actually fails the runbook before /Applications/Plow.app
      gets clobbered. The TeamIdentifier check alone reads the signature
      blob without validating resource hashes.
    - plow-dev-install Step 0a: clear SIGN_TEAM in the ad-hoc fallback so
      Step 4 takes the right verification branch; drop CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED
      = NO so Xcode actually produces an ad-hoc signature instead of leaving
      the bundle unsigned (knightwatch #3, copilot inline).
    - plow-dev-install Step 4: compute the app path the same way the just
      recipe does — `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` instead of hardcoded
      plow3, and the variant-suffixed effective_app_name instead of grepping
      PLOW_APP_NAME from App.xcconfig (which strips internal spaces and
      ignores the variant suffix) (knightwatch #3).
    - Both skills Step 0a: probe `security find-identity` up front so a
      locked / missing keychain bails loudly. Drop the `2>/dev/null` and
      `|| true` that previously masked real failures as "no cert"
      (knightwatch security finding, copilot inline).
    - plow-prod-install: `app/Justfile` → `app/justfile` (case-sensitive
      filesystems) (copilot inline).
    - test_release_scripts.py: pin the new PLOW_CODE_SIGN_IDENTITY contract
      in app/justfile so a future revert to unconditional `-` or
      unconditional ad-hoc post-resign fails CI (knightwatch #4).
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

    fix(imessage): make stopped MessageStoreBridge observable (#501)
    
    * fix(imessage): make stopped MessageStoreBridge observable
    
    When the iMessages permission is off, MessageStoreBridge silently doesn't
    run, chat_clone.db is never created, and the agent's plow_imessage_thread
    tool returns a 503 buried in plowd.log. The user sees nothing actionable.
    
    Two loud failures:
    
    - Phoenix: NSLog the start/stop transition in LocalBridgeController.apply
      so app.log captures every applyPermissionSelection call (including the
      silent auto-finalize from InstallerView.finalizePermissionsIfNeeded).
    - plowd: rewrite the IMessageStoreUnavailableError text to name the cause
      (bridge stopped, permission disabled) and the remedy (toggle in Plow
      Settings → Connectors). The agent now relays a useful instruction
      instead of a path-not-found.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * fix(imessage): tighten unavailable wording per review
    
    Three issues from review:
    
    - The error and the DISABLED log named `plow_imessage_sql`, but the real
      tool is `plow_imessage_analytics` — wrong-name remediation hint.
    - "MessageStoreBridge is not running, which means the iMessages permission
      is disabled" over-claims: bridge.start() schedules the first sync async,
      so there is a brief startup window where the file is genuinely missing
      while permission is on. Same shape applies to bridge.stop(), which only
      cancels the timer; queued sync work can still recreate the clone.
    - The DISABLED log line predicted downstream consequences ("chat_clone.db
      will not be maintained ... will fail") that aren't strictly accurate
      given the stop()-vs-pending-work race, and aren't what an operator
      needs from a lifecycle log line anyway.
    
    Trim to facts: drop the consequence speculation from the log line, soften
    "which means" to "most likely cause", drop the wrong tool list, and add a
    "retry in ~10s for first sync" hint that covers the legitimate startup
    race. Net: shorter, accurate, still actionable.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * fix(imessage): drop unfounded "~10s" hint from unavailable message
    
    Review on #501 flagged that the hardcoded "~10s" retry hint isn't backed by
    any readiness signal — `MessageStoreBridge.start()` only kicks off async
    sync work, and `createClone()` runs a full SQLite backup with no completion
    estimate surfaced across the seam. On a small store the user might wait
    seconds; on an 800MB+ chat.db the first sync can take 30s+. Replace with a
    non-timed hint that doesn't promise a window we can't honor.
    
    Companion to #514 which adds actual bridge-state visibility for a more
    accurate diagnostic; this is the simpler wording fix that stands alone.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * fix(imessage): drop clone path from user-facing 503 detail
    
    Review on #501 flagged that `_unavailable_message()` includes the full
    local clone path, which propagates verbatim through plowd's 503 detail
    into the CLI and OpenClaw tool errors — exposing the macOS username and
    local filesystem layout to the model with no recovery value (the user
    can't act on the path; the actionable hint is the Plow Settings toggle).
    
    Path is constant per-config, so removing it from the per-error message
    does not lose any diagnostic signal an operator wouldn't already have.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
