commit 505e90f2a6cb133a35ac09583ed9493461c19ab6
Author: swagatpatel <swagatpatel@users.noreply.github.com>
Date:   Tue Apr 21 11:38:08 2026 -0700

    Camoufox: fix fallback URL, protocol naming, require path; add xvfb + headless=false (#370)
    
    * fix(camoufox): activate stealth features, fix broken fallback URL, add xvfb
    
    Camoufox was running as a stock headless Firefox — none of the anti-detection
    features were activated. This change:
    
    Phase 1 — Bug fixes:
    - Fix broken fallback URL: add missing /camoufox wsPath to config.py and
      openclaw-start.sh defaults (handshake would fail without it)
    - Fix fragile require path: use require('playwright-core') with NODE_PATH
      instead of hard-coded /usr/local/lib/node_modules path
    - Fix misleading protocol naming: update comments/logs from "CDP" to
      "Playwright ws" across Swift, init, and Python files
    
    Phase 2 — Stealth activation:
    - Switch from headless: true to headless: false (headless mode is detected
      by bot protection via rendering APIs and stack traces)
    - Install xvfb in Gatekeeper VM and wrap launcher with xvfb-run for
      virtual display support in non-headless mode
    
    Phase 3 — Reliability:
    - Extend Playwright startup timeout from 30s to 60s for cold starts
    - Upgrade failure messages from WARNING to ERROR for better diagnostics
    - Add mkdir -p for config/gateway before writing ws endpoint
    
    Co-Authored-By: Paperclip <noreply@paperclip.ing>
    
    * fix(camoufox): address review feedback on boot race, failure surfacing, and env var rename
    
    - Move readiness signal (vsock 18790) in Gatekeeper init to fire only after
      Camoufox ws endpoint is written; exit 1 on launch failure so host sees it
      loudly rather than booting a half-broken VM
    - Write /config/gateway/camoufox-failed sentinel with reason (binary-missing,
      playwright-exited-early, playwright-timeout-60s) on failure paths
    - Capture node's PID directly (drop tee pipeline) so the early-exit kill -0
      check watches the right process and fast-fails instead of stalling 60s
    - Rename env var CAMOUFOX_CDP_URL -> CAMOUFOX_WS_URL in openclaw-start.sh to
      match the ws:// protocol reality (no downstream consumers of the old name)
    - Surface camoufox-failed sentinel contents in DaemonClient when the gatekeeper
      VM start fails, so the Camoufox failure reason lands in the app log
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * fix(gatekeeper): bump playwright-core to 1.59.1 to match openclaw client
    
    The Playwright wire protocol requires matching minor versions between
    the launchServer process and the connecting client. Openclaw 2026.4.12
    (pinned in plow-imessage) ships playwright-core@1.59.1 transitively,
    while the gatekeeper VM still installed 1.58.2, so skills relying on
    the camoufox ws bridge failed with "server is v1.58 but all installed
    clients are v1.59".
    
    Requires a gatekeeper VM rebuild + manifest.toml SHA refresh to ship.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * fix(gatekeeper): install xauth so xvfb-run can start Camoufox
    
    xvfb-run invokes xauth to generate the X magic cookie; without it the
    Playwright launchServer process exits immediately with "xauth command
    not found", the init script writes the camoufox-failed sentinel and
    exits 1, and the VM kernel-panics before signalling readiness. The xvfb
    addition in 499a18e9 was the half-fix; this is the other half.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    ---------
    
    Co-authored-by: Paperclip <noreply@paperclip.ing>
    Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit f41c72f7019bd72aaedf381cfdb26eeb27bfb264
Author: Sam Odio <sam@odio.email>
Date:   Mon Apr 20 10:35:13 2026 -0700

    refactor(plowd): remove dead code from sleep/wake PR series (#368)
    
    * refactor(plowd): remove dead code from sleep/wake PR series (#353–#366)
    
    Three dead code removals surfaced by diffing the sleep/wake PR series
    against pre-353 main:
    
    1. gateway_operator.py:237 — `_DeviceSignatureExpired` in isinstance
       check inside `except Exception`. PR #353 added a dedicated
       `except _DeviceSignatureExpired` clause above it; the type can never
       reach this branch. The adjacent comment even said so. Changed to
       `type(exc) in _GATEWAY_RETRY_REASONS`.
    
    2. gateway.py / main.py — `connector_tools_content` was fetched via a
       real `asyncio.to_thread(fetch_tools_manifest, ...)` network call in
       main.py, passed to `gateway_manager.start()`, and immediately
       `del`-ed on the first line of `start()`. The fetch, the variable, the
       kwarg, and the `del` are all removed. The now-unused
       `fetch_tools_manifest` import is also dropped.
    
    `device_family=""` in `_build_device_auth_payload` (always empty,
    always contributes a trailing `|` to the signed payload) is left alone
    — removing it changes the gateway signing protocol, which is not a
    plowd-only change.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * refactor(phoenix): rename default container label to "Plow Starter"
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * refactor(plowd): drop dead fallback in _GATEWAY_RETRY_REASONS lookup
    
    type(exc) in _GATEWAY_RETRY_REASONS already guarantees the key exists,
    so .get() or str(exc) was unreachable.

 app/Phoenix/ActivationState.swift | 15 +++++++++++----

    Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * test: drop unused mock_session_factory pytest wrapper
    
    The @pytest.fixture wrapper had zero current consumers — both call
    sites are unittest.TestCase subclasses that use the plain helper
    _make_mock_session_factory() directly. Per CLAUDE.md, don't
    introduce helpers for 0-2 call sites. Re-add when a pytest-native
    consumer exists (likely in plow-2w6.4+).
    
    Quality review finding on plow-2w6.2.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * test: replace sys.modules guard in _suppress_print_json with fail-fast monkeypatch
    
    The prior fixture used a "if modname in sys.modules" guard which
    was load-order-dependent and presented as scope-limiting but was
    actually a time-bomb — any test that later imported a command
    module would silently inherit the suppression.
    
    Replace with direct monkeypatch.setattr(string_target, ...) which
    fails fast if the module can't be imported. Add explicit scope
    warning to the docstring with concrete opt-out instructions for
    any future test that wants to assert on print_json output.
    
    Quality review finding on plow-2w6.2.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * test: delete 17 import-only dead tests in test_imports.py
    
    Every test was `from x import y` with no assertion. Import failures are
    caught by any test that exercises those modules. Per CLAUDE.md: remove
    low-value helper tests before adding more boilerplate.
    
    Audit tag: 🗑 dead (all 17). api baseline: 552 → 535.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * test: delete 21 SQLAlchemy-metadata tests in test_models.py
    
    All 21 tests inspect mapper metadata (table names, column sets, attribute
    → DB column aliases) via SQLAlchemy inspect(). This tests the framework,
    not the app. Any real regression (renamed column or table) would be caught
    by the hundreds of DB integration tests that actually insert/query rows.
    Per CLAUDE.md rule #2 in the audit.
    
    Audit tag: ⚠️ implementation (20) + ⚠️ implementation (1 test_base_has_auto_id).
    api baseline: 535 → 514.

commit 1d2ff98a5e446ef6a2dcb3a96f2d6a4b6e455e2b
Author: Sam Odio <sam@odio.email>
Date:   Thu Apr 16 21:00:29 2026 -0700

    fix: off-main @Observable mutation in RuntimeDownloadManager (#335)
    
    Fixes a SIGABRT at launch when OpenClaw / Gatekeeper runtime artifacts
    are already cached from a previous install
    (incident 013D7DED-0772-443F-9A6D-92D93849F210).
    
    RuntimeDownloadManager is @Observable. markArtifactVerified mutated
    the backing flags for openclawVMReady / gatekeeperVMReady from the
    co.plow.runtime-download.state stateQueue. SwiftUI's observation
    callbacks fire synchronously on the setter's thread and touch AppKit,
    which throws an NSException — libdispatch's _dispatch_client_callout
    has no handler, so std::terminate aborts.
    
    - Dispatch the flag mutation to main, matching the existing
      publishStatus pattern.
    - Mark the five `var` queue-owned internal fields @ObservationIgnored
      so a future stored-property addition next to them doesn't silently
      reintroduce the bug.
    - Add RuntimeDownloadManagerTests covering the invariant via
      withObservationTracking from a background queue; clears
      PLOW_VM_BUNDLE / PLOW_GATEKEEPER_VM_BUNDLE in setUp so the tracked
      read doesn't short-circuit on dev machines, and NSLock-wraps the
      thread-capture box for TSAN correctness.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 app/Phoenix/RuntimeDownloadManager.swift | 28 ++++++++++++++++++++--------
 1 file changed, 20 insertions(+), 8 deletions(-)

commit 1810e80b7c8862824eb3c230be5fdf791a3e9058
Author: swagatpatel <swagatpatel@users.noreply.github.com>
Date:   Thu Apr 16 11:29:39 2026 -0700

    feat: VM runtime artifact versioning with immutable S3 keys (#297)
    
    * feat: VM runtime artifact versioning (all 7 phases)
    
    Add git ref-based versioning to VM build artifacts with a staging/promote
    deployment model, replacing the previous in-place overwrite workflow.
    
    Phase 1: Build scripts append .<git-short> to tarball names, write git_ref to TOML
    Phase 2: Upload targets runtime-builds/ with overwrite guard; new promote script
    Phase 3: Manifest generator includes git ref in version strings
    Phase 4: CI verifies runtime artifacts exist in S3 before release build
    Phase 5: RuntimeDownloadManager uses versioned cache keys, stale cleanup
    Phase 6: VMBundleSupport uses SHA256 hash comparison instead of file size
    Phase 7: Terraform S3 lifecycle rules (90d staging, versioning enabled)
    
    Co-Authored-By: Paperclip <noreply@paperclip.ing>
    
    * fix: address CTO review feedback on VM versioning
    
    1. Use bash 3.2-safe ${aws_args[@]+...} expansion in upload and promote
       scripts to avoid unbound variable errors when AWS_PROFILE is unset
    2. Write .rootfs-identity after extraction so VMBundleSupport reads the
       pre-computed hash instead of re-hashing ~400MB rootfs on first launch
    3. Use .tar. range search instead of hardcoded 8-char drop in stale
       cache cleanup
    4. Clarify promote script comment re: git-ref stripping
    
    Co-Authored-By: Paperclip <noreply@paperclip.ing>
    
    * fix: exclude VM manifests from git dirty check during builds
    
    When `just app runtime build` runs both VM builds sequentially, the first
    build writes metadata to its manifest.toml, causing the second build to
    see the tree as dirty and incorrectly append `-dirty` to its artifact name.
    Exclude both manifest files from the git diff check.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Paperclip <noreply@paperclip.ing>
    
    * fix: address PR review — cache cleanup, promote naming, stale manifest
    
    1. Cache cleanup deletes current tarballs: cleanupStaleCacheEntries()
       used baseName extraction that missed the .zst extension on downloaded
       tarballs. Switched to prefix matching so both the extract directory
       and the tarball file are preserved.
    
    2. Staging/promote naming inconsistency: promote-runtime-artifacts.sh
       stripped the git ref when copying to production, but the manifest
       generator and CI release gate both check the full git-ref-suffixed
       artifact name. Promote now preserves the full artifact name.
    
    3. Stale bundled runtime-manifest.json: just release now regenerates
       runtime-manifest.json before building so release builds always have
       current checksums. Updated the bundled manifest to include the new
       gitRef field.
    
    Also: build scripts now clean up Docker build context artifacts via trap
    on early exit, preventing untracked files like openclaw-2026.4.12.tgz.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Paperclip <noreply@paperclip.ing>
    
    * simplify: remove staging/promote split, use single runtime/ prefix
    
    With versioned immutable filenames (git ref baked in), the staging/promote
    split is unnecessary ceremony. An artifact in S3 that no app references is
    just unreleased — the app bundle's baked-in URL is the release pointer.
    
    - Delete promote-runtime-artifacts.sh and `just runtime promote` action
    - Upload directly to runtime/ (not runtime-builds/)
    - Remove Terraform lifecycle rule for the now-unused runtime-builds/ prefix
    - Keep: overwrite guard, CI head-object gate, S3 versioning safety net
    
    Co-Authored-By: Paperclip <noreply@paperclip.ing>
    
    * docs: update vm-publish and vm-local-setup for versioned artifact workflow
    
    - vm-publish.md: rewritten for git-ref artifact naming, single runtime/
      prefix (no staging/promote), overwrite guard, CI release gate, release
      manifest auto-regen, .rootfs-identity marker, stale cache cleanup
    - vm-local-setup.md: updated manifest schema to include gitRef field,
      fixed stale .manifest-hash reference
    
    Co-Authored-By: Paperclip <noreply@paperclip.ing>
    
    * fix: remove orphaned aws_s3_bucket_versioning resource
    
    The lifecycle rule that consumed versioning was removed in dda9fc58 but the
    versioning resource itself was left behind. Applying it would enable S3
    versioning bucket-wide (a one-way door) with no cleanup policy, accumulating
    noncurrent versions on every overwrite forever.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Paperclip <noreply@paperclip.ing>
    
    * chore: remove s3-releases.tf — no terraform changes needed in this PR
    
    The releases bucket already exists and needs no new terraform resources.
    Versioning/lifecycle config was added then iteratively removed; drop the
    file entirely per board feedback.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Paperclip <noreply@paperclip.ing>
    
    * docs: add explicit release prerequisite section to vm-publish.md
    
    The CI release workflow validates that VM artifacts exist in S3 but
    does not build or deploy them itself. This was implicit in the TL;DR
    but not called out as a hard prerequisite. Added a clear "Release
    prerequisite" section spelling out the required sequence.

commit 7f7a893107762c44bb79ff0fe0821a988228db4f
Author: Sam Odio <sam@odio.email>
Date:   Thu Apr 16 08:00:39 2026 -0700

    fix(llm-auth): purge dead Codex profile on auth-error + OpenAI auth UX (#296)
    
    * fix(plow-imessage): rewrite leaked Codex auth error before shipping to chat
    
    When a user switches the agent's LLM provider to openai-codex/gpt-5.4
    via the Plow UI but the stored Codex OAuth profile is missing/expired/
    revoked, the agent's SDK call fails with the literal upstream error
    "Encountered invalidated oauth token for user, failing request" and
    openclaw currently emits that raw string as the assistant message.
    The user sees a cryptic internal error in their iMessage thread with
    no actionable guidance.
    
    Intercept at the last hop before the reply leaves the VM (same layer
    as the existing `stripHashGateComment` rewrite) and replace any reply
    that is entirely this error with a user-facing fallback that:
    
    1. Explains the provider login has expired
    2. Gives the exact re-auth command (matches `plowd/main.py:834`)
    3. Offers the escape hatch of switching back to Claude in the Plow UI
    
    The detection regex is anchored start-to-end so the rewrite only fires
    when the *entire* reply is the bare error — an agent quoting the error
    inside a longer explanation passes through untouched.
    
    Observed in chat_1 msg_785 on slowdown while dogfooding the openclaw
    VM rootfs + channel-dir fix (PR #295); the provider switch happened
    right before the user's next inbound message, so the very next agent
    LLM call went to the Codex OAuth path and failed.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * fix(phoenix): show re-auth button when Codex OAuth token is expired
    
    Phoenix already had the full click-to-re-auth path wired up
    (`DaemonClient.startOpenAIOAuth` posts to the plowd OAuth kickoff
    endpoint, opens the consent URL in Safari via `NSWorkspace.shared.open`,
    and polls for completion), but `LLMProviderSection` was gating it on
    `!connected`, which meant the button only showed up for missing
    profiles — never for expired ones.
    
    When Codex server-side invalidated the stored OAuth token, plowd's
    status loader (`plowd/main.py:1130`) correctly reported
    `{connected: true, status: "expired"}` (profile file still on disk,
    just with a dead token). Phoenix saw `connected: true` and:
    
    - Kept "GPT 5.4 (your OpenAI)" selectable in the dropdown
    - Never showed the "Connect OpenAI" re-auth button
    - Gave the user zero indication anything was wrong
    
    The next agent message then blew up with the literal upstream error
    `"Encountered invalidated oauth token for user, failing request"`,
    which leaked verbatim into the iMessage thread (fixed separately in
    PR #296 via a rewrite at the last hop out of the VM).
    
    Fix: teach `isAvailable()`, the dropdown unavailability suffix, and the
    re-auth button visibility to all react to `status == "expired"`, not
    just `!connected`. Button label toggles between "Connect OpenAI" and
    "Re-authenticate OpenAI" so the user can tell the two states apart.
    
    Also update the `plow-imessage` channel's fallback text (PR #296) to
    point at this Phoenix button instead of a Terminal command — since
    the Phoenix UI is now the one-click path and plowd binds only to
    `127.0.0.1`, a clickable URL in iMessage wouldn't work cross-device
    (iPhone-iMessage can't reach Mac localhost) so the fallback reply
    steers the user at the menu bar app where re-auth actually works.
    
    No Swift unit test added — `LLMProviderSection` is a SwiftUI view with
    no existing test target precedent in the Phoenix bundle, and the
    branching logic in `isAvailable`/`needsOpenAIReauth`/`openaiButtonLabel`
    is straight-line against strongly-typed `ProviderStatus` fields.
    The plowd-side source of truth (`test_model_auth_endpoints.py`) already
    verifies the `/internal/openclaw/models/auth/status` endpoint returns
    `{status: "expired"}` when the profile's `expires` timestamp is in the
    past, so the contract this commit depends on is covered.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * fix(plow-imessage): catch second Codex auth-error variant too
    
    Observed a new variant of the leaked Codex error on 2026-04-15:
    
        "Your authentication token has been invalidated. Please try
         signing in again."
    
    Emitted in chat_1 a few minutes after the "Encountered invalidated
    oauth token for user, failing request" string the first regex caught.
    Different Codex SDK code paths raise with different phrasings, so
    the existing single-regex guard in PR #296 let this one through
    unchanged.
    
    Refactor `CODEX_INVALIDATED_OAUTH_RE` into a `CODEX_AUTH_ERROR_PATTERNS`
    list of anchored regexes and check any-match. Keeping them as a list
    (rather than one broad `invalidated.*token` heuristic) is deliberate:
    
    - Easy to extend when we find variant #3
    - Can't clobber legitimate agent replies that happen to discuss auth
    
    Added a unit test mirroring the existing one for the original string.
    All 26 tests pass; bundle rebuilt and grepped to confirm both variants
    are present in dist/index.js.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * fix(plow-imessage): purge dead Codex profile on auth-error detection
    
    Simpler re-architecture of PR #296. Instead of teaching Phoenix new
    provider states (expired vs not_connected), exploit the re-auth path
    Phoenix ALREADY has — which only fires on `!connected`.
    
    When the channel detects a known Codex auth error leaking as the
    agent's reply (the existing `rewriteProviderAuthError` detection),
    we now ALSO delete the dead profile from:
    
        ${OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR}/agents/main/agent/auth-profiles.json
    
    That makes plowd's next `/internal/openclaw/models/auth/status` poll
    return `{connected: false, status: "not_connected"}`, which makes
    Phoenix's existing "Connect OpenAI" button surface on the next menu
    bar open. The user's one-click path back to a working agent is:
    
        see fallback reply → open Plow menu bar → Connect OpenAI →
          approve in Safari → fresh profile written → try again
    
    ## Why the channel does this instead of plowd or openclaw
    
    OpenAI server-side revocation leaves the local profile looking valid
    (access JWT + refresh token both present, `expires` still hours or
    days in the future), so plowd's local-timestamp check (`main.py:1128`
    `is_expired = expires > 0 and expires < now_ms`) keeps reporting
    `status: "active"`. openclaw's in-memory cooldown knows the profile
    is dead but never propagates the state back to disk.
    
    The channel is the first component in the outbound path that actually
    *observes* the upstream rejection — it sees the raw error string the
    agent tried to ship as its reply — so it's the right place to act on
    it.
    
    ## Why this reverts the Phoenix changes from 4b20151c
    
    PR #296 added `StatusView.swift` handling for `status == "expired"`
    as a workaround for the fact that nothing on disk ever transitioned
    to that state. With this commit, the channel moves the profile to
    the not-connected state Phoenix already handles, so the expired-state
    UI hooks become dead code. Reverted to the original `!connected`
    check to keep the Phoenix surface area minimal.
    
    ## Test plan
    
    - [x] `npx tsc --noEmit` clean
    - [x] `npx vitest run send.test.ts` — all existing tests pass plus 6
          new `purgeCodexAuthProfileBestEffort` tests (happy path, key vs
          provider-field matching, no-op on clean file, silent bail on
          unset env, silent bail on missing file, end-to-end via
          `sendMessage`)
    - [x] `npm run build` emits cleanly; grepped dist/index.js to confirm
          both `purgeCodexAuthProfileBestEffort` and the new fallback
          text land in the bundle
    - [ ] Manual end-to-end on a real VM: trigger a Codex auth failure,
          confirm the reply shows the fallback AND the Plow menu bar now
          offers "Connect OpenAI". Needs a VM rebuild + channel redeploy.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * refactor(plow-imessage): slim auth-error handling to ~60 lines total
    
    Collapse the detection / rewrite / purge machinery into one function
    `handleLeakedAuthError()` that does all three, and trim the test suite
    to three focused cases.
    
    Before (PR #296 as-of fa4be4e4):
    
        send.ts      +175 LOC  — 3 exports, giant JSDoc on each
        send.test.ts +242 LOC  — 3 describe blocks, 13 new tests
        total        +417 LOC
    
    After:
    
        send.ts      ~35 LOC   — 1 export, one paragraph of comment
        send.test.ts ~50 LOC   — 1 describe block, 3 tests
        total        ~85 LOC
    
    Same behavior, same bundle output. Specifically:
    
    - `CODEX_AUTH_ERROR_PATTERNS` array of 2 regexes → 1 regex with
      alternation (anchored the same way).
    - `rewriteProviderAuthError` + `PROVIDER_AUTH_ERROR_FALLBACK` +
      `purgeCodexAuthProfileBestEffort` + the sendMessage wiring →
      one `handleLeakedAuthError(text): Promise<string | null>` that
      detects, purges, and returns either the fallback reply or null.
    - Dropped the atomic-rename / tmp-file-cleanup error path — a plain
      `writeFile` is fine for a 2KB file under best-effort semantics.
    - Dropped match-by-both-key-and-provider-field — the provider field
      is the source of truth; if a weird-keyed profile has the wrong
      provider field, we have bigger problems.
    - Tests cut from 13 to 3: detection (both variants + one negative
      case), and one end-to-end that covers the whole pipeline via
      `sendMessage`.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit 759d780faa35f32002836b5d2a53bb16d70de714
Author: Sam Odio <sam@odio.email>
Date:   Wed Apr 15 21:54:38 2026 -0700

    fix(dev): fall back to app bundle cli-packages when staging dir is stale (#311)
    
    In dev mode, resolveCliPackagesRootForMounts() only checked the staging
    directory (app/plowd/runtime/stage/cli-packages). plowd-stage-runtime
    wipes this directory on every run (rm -rf stage_dir), so between builds
    the cli-packages subdirectory can be missing. When this happens, the VM
    boots with an empty virtiofs mount and the plow CLI is unavailable —
    the agent reports "the underlying plow CLI isn't available here" and
    plow_cp silently fails.
    
    Now checks the staging directory first (for live iteration), then falls
    back to the packaged copy in the app bundle (always present after a
    successful build).
    
    Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 app/Phoenix/DaemonClient.swift | 26 ++++++++++++++++++--------
 1 file changed, 18 insertions(+), 8 deletions(-)

 app/Phoenix/Resources/runtime-manifest.json | 8 ++++----

commit 18103e862e451bcf4a218d2e86b5a55528baee4b
Author: Sam Odio <sam@odio.email>
Date:   Wed Apr 15 15:41:31 2026 -0700

    fix(openclaw-vm): grow rootfs to 3G + point channel dir at dist/ (#295)
    
    * fix(openclaw-vm): grow rootfs to 3G + point channel dir at dist/
    
    Two stacked bugs were silently keeping the dev VM from booting OpenClaw
    on freshly-rebuilt installs. The Phoenix UI hung at "Plow Offline …
    Installing…" indefinitely.
    
    ## Bug 1: rootfs is full
    
    `build-vm-image` resizes the ext4 image down to 2G after baking the
    container fs. `openclaw 2026.4.12` ships ~1.2GB of vendored deps under
    `node_modules/openclaw`; combined with the base OS + Node runtime the
    total `/usr` tree pushes rootfs usage to ~1.9GB, leaving 0 bytes free
    for `/tmp`. OpenClaw's per-session `mkdir /tmp/openclaw-<pid>` at boot
    fails with ENOSPC and the gateway never starts. Bumped the shrink
    target to 3G so `/tmp` has ~1GB of headroom. The 4G create / shrink
    dance is preserved — only the final size changed.
    
    ## Bug 2: channel plugin path missing index.js
    
    `PLOWD_OPENCLAW_CHANNEL_DIR` was set to `/plow-imessage` (the plugin
    root), but esbuild emits the bundled `index.js` into
    `/plow-imessage/dist/` along with the copied `openclaw.plugin.json`.
    OpenClaw's plugin loader requires `index.js` and `openclaw.plugin.json`
    to be siblings in the same directory — the working plugins
    (`/plugin`, `/plow-cli-plugin`) happen to ship that way, but the
    TypeScript-built channel had an extra `dist/` level.
    
    The symptom was a misleading "channels.plow-imessage: unknown channel
    id: plow-imessage" gateway error, which made it look like a config or
    plugin-id mismatch when in fact the loader just couldn't find the
    entrypoint at the path it was told to load from.
    
    Pointed both `DaemonClient.swift` call sites at `/plow-imessage/dist`
    and updated the two plowd tests that mocked the old path.
    
    ## manifest.toml
    
    Updated `openclaw_vm_sha256` / `openclaw_vm_size` to reflect the new
    3G-rootfs build artifact. The local `app/vm/output/` tarball already
    matches; deploying to S3 still needs `just app runtime build deploy`
    before this lands on main, otherwise CI / fresh installs that download
    from S3 will get a sha mismatch against the old cached artifact.
    
    ## Verification
    
    - `pytest app/plowd/tests/{test_activate,test_startup_activation,test_gateway}.py`
      — 66 passed
    - `just app vm-build` — produces 3.0G rootfs.img (up from 1.9G)
    - Full `/plow-activate slowdown` flow — agent replied with intro
      message in chat_1 within ~30s of activation code post (previously
      hung indefinitely on "Plow Offline / Installing…")
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * cleanup: dedupe channel-dir constant + drop dead parent-level plugin.json
    
    - DaemonClient.swift: hoist the /plow-imessage/dist literal + rationale
      into a single `openClawChannelDir` static constant; drop the duplicate
      comment from the two launch paths.
    - plowd-stage-runtime: stop copying openclaw.plugin.json into the parent
      /plow-imessage dir. Nothing loads it there — OpenClaw's loader reads
      the dist/ sibling copy.
    - plowd-package-runtime / plowd-validate-package: swap the parent-level
      plugin.json entry for the dist/ one so packaging + validation match
      the file that actually gets loaded.

commit 45c7f55e1491e109764b275213206e3247b72e76
Author: Sam Odio <sam@odio.email>
Date:   Wed Apr 15 12:53:09 2026 -0700

    Auto-enumerate skills into AGENTS.md + drop dead service_url (#271)
    
    * feat(plowd): auto-enumerate skills into AGENTS.md + drop dead service_url
    
    Two changes that share the same spirit — make agent-facing state flow
    deterministic instead of relying on silent dual sources of truth.
    
    Skills manifest
    ---------------
    Agents were not discovering user-authored workspace skills (e.g. a new
    colton-agent/SKILL.md dropped into /workspace/skills/) even though the
    dashboard reported them eligible. Root cause: AGENTS.md hardcoded three
    skills by name and relied on a vague "scan the skills dir if you think
    you might need something" directive for everything else. That directive
    is a coin flip — it fires only when the incoming request heuristically
    matches a description the agent has not yet seen.
    
    Fix: gateway.write_workspace_guidance() now walks
    ${workspace_dir}/skills/*/SKILL.md, parses frontmatter, and inlines a
    sorted ## Installed Skills section into AGENTS.md on every plowd start.
    Each bullet is "name — description → path", so every installed skill is
    in the system prompt by name. A sha256 skills-manifest-hash HTML comment
    plus a "debug skills" echo directive lets compliance be verified from
    outside the agent — ask "debug skills" in chat and the agent should
    respond with the current hash verbatim.
    
    The template's hardcoded plow-file-discovery / plow-vip-contacts /
    browser-usage lines and the lazy-scan fallback sentence are removed;
    all three now flow through the auto-generated manifest.
    
    Drop dead service_url from setup.json activation
    -------------------------------------------------
    Phoenix's writeActivationToken() was persisting serviceURL into
    setup.json, and _consume_pending_token() was reading it as a fallback
    via `settings.vm_plow_api_url.strip() or service_url`. But Phoenix also
    sets PLOW_VM_PLOW_API_URL in the launcher env every time it spawns
    plowd (DaemonClient.swift:2401,2463), using the same
    ServiceURLs.vmPlowAPIURL() value. In production, settings.vm_plow_api_url
    is always non-empty — so the `or service_url` branch was dead code and
    the setup.json field was pure redundancy.
    
    Fix: drop PhoenixActivationSetup.serviceURL entirely. plowd reads
    settings.vm_plow_api_url directly and logs + aborts cleanly if it is
    empty (meaning the launcher env is broken). One source of truth: Phoenix
    resolves the VM API URL, writes it to the process env, plowd reads it
    from the env.
    
    Drive-by fixes to 3 pre-existing test_gateway.py assertions that still
    asserted tmp_path would appear in rendered AGENTS.md — stale since
    default_workspace_dir was pinned to /workspace.
    
    Test state
    ----------
    - app/plowd just test: 337 passed, 7 failed (all 7 pre-existing failures
      live in test_daemon_client_source.py, test_release_scripts.py, and
      test_runtime_distribution.py — stale string-match and stale fixture
      tests from Swift refactors and runtime-artifact refactors, documented
      separately, none touch anything in this branch).
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * fix(plowd): use on-disk directory slug for SKILL.md path in manifest
    
    The ## Installed Skills manifest was emitting SKILL.md paths using the
    frontmatter `name:` field, which defeats the purpose in the exact case
    the feature targets: a user authoring a new skill whose frontmatter name
    doesn't match the directory slug. Example: a skill at
    `/workspace/skills/my-skill/SKILL.md` with `name: "My Fancy Skill"`
    would have produced a broken
    `/workspace/skills/My Fancy Skill/SKILL.md` path, so the agent could
    never read the actual skill file.
    
    Fix: carry the on-disk directory slug (`skill_dir.name`) alongside
    parsed frontmatter and use it for both the path and the display label.
    Sort by slug too, so ordering is deterministic based on filesystem
    truth rather than author-controlled frontmatter.
    
    Caught in review by Copilot on PR #271.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * feat(skills): add daily-rampup reference skill
    
    A user-authored showcase skill that demonstrates the auto-enumeration
    flow from the skill-manifest work in this branch. Lives in the
    templates dir as a reference copy; users install it by dropping the
    file into their live workspace at
    `<app-support>/openclaw/gateway/workspace/skills/daily-rampup/`.
    
    On plowd builds that include the skill-manifest feature, the skill
    will appear in the `## Installed Skills` block of AGENTS.md on next
    start, visible to the agent by name from turn 1. On older builds
    without the feature, the skill is still discoverable via the existing
    lazy-scan directive or by explicit path reference.
    
    Skill body describes: overnight top-contact iMessages, unread Gmail
    highlights, optional calendar pass, and a single "one thing to watch"
    item, packaged as an iMessage-friendly brief capped at ~200 words.

    * fix(plowd): address skill-manifest review nits
    
    Three followups from the post-approval self-review on PR #271.
    
    1. Drop redundant entries.sort in _build_installed_skills_section.
       `sorted(skills_dir.iterdir())` already yields subdirs in alphabetical
       order and the slug is set from `skill_dir.name` in that same loop,
       so the downstream `entries.sort(key=slug)` was dead work on every
       plowd start. Replaced with a comment explaining the guarantee.
    
    2. Skip dot-prefix directories. A `.git` checkout or `.DS_Store`-style
       directory under `workspace/skills/` would previously be scanned
       (and, if it happened to contain a parseable SKILL.md, picked up
       into the manifest). Add an explicit guard so hidden dirs never
       enter the enumeration, regardless of contents. Includes regression
       test that plants a parseable SKILL.md inside `.git` and asserts
       it's excluded from the manifest.
    
    3. Lock frontmatter parser contract with edge-case tests. The regex
       parser's behavior on realistic YAML edges was underspecified; add
       two tests that lock in current behavior — quoted values with
       internal colons (`description: "time: 10:00"`) are preserved
       intact, and indented fields nested under a parent block are
       rejected (only column-0 top-level keys are matched). Expand the
       `_parse_skill_frontmatter` docstring to state the contract
       explicitly, including the known gap around multi-line YAML block
       scalars (`description: >` / `|`).
    
    Verified via `just test` in app/plowd: 410 passed, 7 pre-existing
    failures in unrelated test modules (daemon_client_source, release_scripts,
    runtime_distribution — all predate this branch).

    * fix(workspace-template): drop HTML comment advice from skill gate
    
    The previous directive told the agent to include the hash in "a hidden
    trailing HTML comment", but the iMessage channel sends text verbatim
    and Apple Messages renders HTML comments as literal text — the comment
    would leak into the user's message thread. The forcing function only
    needs the shell-tool invocation of plow skill print to happen; the
    hash ending up in the reply was never load-bearing. Drop the advice,
    tighten the prose, and unwrap to match surrounding AGENTS.md style.

    * fix(plow-cli): print full skill body, stop silently dropping content
    
    Final branch review caught that _extract_key_highlights used a
    hardcoded 5-heading whitelist designed around daily-rampup's section
    names. On plow-file-discovery and browser-usage, only "## Rules"
    matched — silently dropping 60–89% of the operational contract
    (plow_pull_file sync-vs-delivery semantics, the ws Camoufox endpoint
    and firefox.connect example, etc). Agents reading those skills via
    plow skill print would get the negative-constraint rules without
    the operational model to actually invoke the tool.

    _strip_frontmatter helper that removes the YAML frontmatter header
    and returns the full remaining body. The hash is still the forcing
    function — it's obtained via shell exec, which proves the file was
    read. Condensation was solving a problem that doesn't exist at
    current skill sizes (1.5–4 KB each).

    Add a parameterized regression test that runs plow skill print
    against every shipped first-party skill and asserts load-bearing
    operational strings are present in the output.

    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * bd init: initialize beads issue tracking
    
    * feat(plowd): compute per-skill partial hash for installed skills manifest
    
    Reintroduce hashlib (retired in 37eddb81) to compute
    sha256(skill_file_bytes).hexdigest()[:8] for each skill installed under
    workspace/skills/. Emit the prefix in each ## Installed Skills bullet
    as a 🔐 partial-hash marker. This is the routing-table layer of an
    arsenal-style hash gate — the agent cannot produce a valid full-hash
    response token without running `plow skill print`, and the prefix in
    the bullet is what its response hash must match. The gate directive
    prose will be wired into the AGENTS.md template in the next commit.
    
    New helper _compute_skill_file_prefix takes a Path to SKILL.md and
    returns the first SKILL_HASH_PREFIX_LEN (=8) hex chars of the sha256,
    or None if the file is unreadable. Eight hex chars = 32 bits of
    entropy, comfortably collision-free for a handful of skills.
    
    Tests:
    - test_build_installed_skills_section_emits_partial_hash_prefix asserts
      each bullet carries a backticked 8-hex-char prefix after the 🔐
      marker.
    - test_build_installed_skills_section_partial_hash_matches_sha256_of_file
      documents the contract: the prefix equals sha256(raw_bytes)[:8]
      computed directly from the file, and _compute_skill_file_prefix
      returns the same value.
    - test_build_installed_skills_section_partial_hash_changes_when_body_changes
      confirms content-sensitivity — any edit invalidates the prefix.
    - test_build_installed_skills_section_retires_canary_echo_directive is
      the rename of the old "no longer embeds full hash" test, which was
      misleadingly named since we now DO embed a partial. Its assertions
      still hold: no skills-manifest-hash HTML comment, no debug-skills
      echo directive.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * feat(workspace-template): require full-hash echo as arsenal-style gate
    
    Rewrite the ## Using Installed Skills directive to use the partial-hash
    puzzle as the forcing function, not a MUST-run directive. The previous
    version told the agent to run plow skill print before acting — which
    worked via prompt compliance but left no visible proof in the reply.
    
    New contract:
    
    1. Every skill bullet shows a partial-hash prefix (🔐 cb0a630e) — the
       first 8 chars of the skill file's sha256, computed at manifest
       render time by _build_installed_skills_section.
    2. When the user's request matches a skill, the agent MUST start its
       reply with a line like "🔐 <slug>: <64-char-sha256>", and the first
       8 chars of the echoed hash MUST match the prefix shown.
    3. The only way to obtain the full hash is to run plow skill print
       <path>, which reads the file — and the command also prints the
       skill body the agent needs to follow. Reading the file is the side
       effect; echoing the hash is the proof.
    4. Faking the hash doesn't work: sha256 is deterministic, the prefix
       is derivable from the file on disk, and any reviewer can re-verify
       compliance by grepping the reply.
    
    Compliance is now visible in the response text itself (the 🔐 line at
    the top of the reply), not buried in shell-tool exec logs. Arsenal's
    bootstrap-token pattern, ported to Plow's dynamic manifest.
    
    The example directive uses daily-rampup's current hash prefix
    (cb0a630e) so the format is obvious. The integration test
    test_write_workspace_guidance_uses_plow_owned_agents_template now
    asserts the new directive text and regexes over first-party bullets
    to confirm each has an 8-hex 🔐 prefix.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * feat(hash-gate): wrap compliance marker in HTML comment, strip on outbound
    
    Pivot the visible format of the skill-gate compliance marker from a
    plain-text "🔐 <slug>: <hash>" line to an HTML comment
    "<!-- 🔐 hash-gate <slug> <hash> -->", and strip it on the outbound
    path of the plow-imessage channel before the reply leaves the VM.
    
    The forcing function is unchanged: the agent still has to run
    `plow skill print <path>` to obtain the full sha256, whose first 8
    chars must match the partial prefix shown in the `## Installed Skills`
    manifest bullet. What's new is the UX — end users no longer see the
    gate line in their iMessage thread, but dev dogfooding still does
    (the strip is skipped when PLOW_LAUNCH_MODE=dev).
    
    The channel-side strip lives in stripHashGateComment() in send.ts
    (exported for unit testability) and runs as the first step inside
    sendMessage() before the outbound fetch. Fail-closed semantics: any
    PLOW_LAUNCH_MODE value other than "dev" — including the env var being
    unset — runs the strip, so accidental misconfiguration cannot leak
    the gate to production users. Regex matches any HTML comment whose
    body contains the literal `hash-gate` keyword, permissive by design
    so future tweaks to the marker (version strings, priority tags) don't
    silently bypass the strip.
    
    Bullet format in `## Installed Skills` drops the 🔐 emoji (since the
    bullets themselves are invisible to end users — AGENTS.md is
    agent-only) and switches to middle-dot separation:
    
        - `cb0a630e` · `daily-rampup` — Morning ramp-up brief … → …
    
    AGENTS.md gate directive rewritten to describe the new comment
    format, the MUST-echo rule, the dev/prod visibility split, and a
    concrete example using daily-rampup's real current hash.
    
    Tests:
    - send.test.ts: 10 new cases for stripHashGateComment covering top-of-reply
      strip, emoji-less variant, multi-line comment, unrelated comments passed
      through, no-op when absent, dev-mode pass-through, non-dev modes strip,
      fail-closed on missing env var, multiple gates in one reply.
    - Two new sendMessage integration cases: strip happens in prod mode,
      preserves in dev mode.
    - test_gateway.py: updated _build_installed_skills_section assertions for
      the middle-dot separator and no 🔐 in bullets. Integration test
      test_write_workspace_guidance_uses_plow_owned_agents_template now
      asserts the new directive text (hash-gate HTML comment format,
      PLOW_LAUNCH_MODE=dev mention, concrete daily-rampup example) and
      regexes the first-party bullets for the middle-dot format.
    
    Verified:
    - cd app/openclaw/channels/plow-imessage && npm test → 52 passed
    - cd app/plowd && just test → 413 passed, 7 pre-existing failures
      (all unrelated, same set tracked all session)
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * chore(skill-manifest): self-review cleanups on PR #271
    
    Three cosmetic cleanups from a self-review of the skills-manifest PR,
    all non-behavioral except the frontmatter quote-stripping:
    
    - send.ts: drop redundant `\n?` at end of HASH_GATE_RE (`\s*` already
      matches newlines). Pure noise, behavior is unchanged — the trailer
      was consuming whitespace twice.
    
    - AGENTS.md template: soften "you cannot fake the hash" to "the gate
      is a forcing function, not a cryptographic proof." Accurate — an
      agent with any file-read tool can compute sha256 without going
      through `plow skill print`. The real mechanism is that producing a
      matching hash requires touching the file, which is exactly the
      intent (load the body into context, not prove cryptographically).
      Avoids overclaiming a guarantee the gate doesn't provide.
    
    - gateway.py `_parse_skill_frontmatter`: strip a single matched pair
      of surrounding `"` or `'` quotes from parsed name/description. The
      previous behavior preserved quotes literally in the manifest bullet,
      which rendered as `` `clock` — "time is 10:00 today" `` — visible
      oddness if a skill author reached for quotes to escape an internal
      colon. Flipped the existing lock-in test
      `test_build_installed_skills_section_preserves_quoted_colon_in_description`
      to assert the new unquoted rendering, covering both `"` and `'`.
    
    Verified: `pytest app/plowd/tests/test_gateway.py` (58 passed) and
    `npm test -- --run src/send.test.ts` (18 passed).

commit 6100968b130cb77f5d67239d9ba984d0248b8c4f
Author: Sam Odio <sam@odio.email>
Date:   Wed Apr 15 12:12:11 2026 -0700

    fix: show pretty Slack team name in connector UI (#290)
    
    PR #269 added a single-element accounts[] for Slack so the dashboard
    could render "connected", but AccountInfo only carried `account`
    (the raw `team_id/user_id`), not `display_name`. Both the dashboard
    and the Phoenix settings row fell off the display_name fallback path
    and started rendering the raw identifier.
    
    Add display_name to AccountInfo, populate it from
    token.extra["display_name"] in both /v1/connectors and
    /v1/connectors/{name}/status, and render it (falling back to the raw
    account) in the web dashboard and the Phoenix connector row.

    * build: auto-invalidate stale app/vm/output on just app up

    wipe the dir so Phoenix downloads fresh from S3 instead of silently
    using stale bits via the PLOW_VM_BUNDLE auto-export. Same check for
    gatekeeper. Without this, the accompanying openclaw 3.12→4.12 bump
    would break setups for any dev who had built a 3.12 VM locally.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * build: regenerate plow-imessage lockfile under Node 22 / npm 10
    
    The previous regen happened on Node 25 / npm 11, which omits some
    optional peer deps (opusscript, etc) from the top-level lockfile
    entries. CI runs Node 22 (npm 10), which is stricter and rejects the
    lockfile via `npm ci` with "Missing: opusscript@0.0.8 from lock file".
    
    Regen on Node 22.18.0 / npm 10.9.3 to match CI. Same package count
    (767 vs 766 — one extra top-level entry). Doesn't pin Node version
    across the repo — that's the broader concern reverted #257 tried to
    solve, follow-up worthy.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * docs: correct vm-openclaw-upgrade deep-surface claims
    
    The "Deep compatibility surface" section + risk matrix + Option A probe
    all asserted that runtime.channel.* calls were implicit-any against a
    local shim and that tsc wouldn't catch their shape changes. That's
    wrong — openclaw ships complete .d.ts files in node_modules, tsc
    resolves against them via package.json exports, and PluginRuntimeChannel
    fully types the runtime.channel.* chain. The 4.12 upgrade confirmed
    this (CI caught all three import breaks).
    
    The ambient shim at types/openclaw/plugin-sdk.d.ts exists but is dead
    code (not in tsconfig include glob). Leaving the file in place per
    user decision; doc now calls it out as dead rather than recommending
    it as a "probe target."
    
    Replaces Option A/B/C with a single unambiguous "dev VM smoke test"
    step. Risk matrix updated so type-shape changes are all CAUGHT EARLY;
    only semantic (same-shape, different-meaning) changes fall through to
    runtime.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    ---------
    
    Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit 1ab9e2b5083e1bdf845a714366bd5080379e8b79
Author: Sam Odio <sam@odio.email>
Date:   Tue Apr 14 03:02:07 2026 -0700

    feat: dashboard connector management + Phoenix deep-link (#269)
    
    * Phoenix: add per-account disconnect + set-default DaemonClient helpers
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * Phoenix: percent-encode '+' in connector account query param
    
    URLComponents.queryItems leaves '+' literal but Starlette decodes it as
    space, so Gmail plus-addresses would be mangled on the backend. Use
    percentEncodedQueryItems with '+' removed from the allowed set.
    
    * Phoenix: per-account Gmail UI with default marker and disconnect/set-default menu
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * Phoenix: drop 'Gmail' from disconnect alert title
    
    The alert fires for any multi-account connector, not just Gmail. The
    message body already shows the full address, so the connector name
    in the title was both redundant and incorrect for any future
    non-Gmail multi-account connector.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * api: expose web_base_url on /v1/connectors response
    
    * Phoenix: remove per-account mutation methods, add dashboardURL helper
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * Phoenix: clarify dashboardURL rationale, mark @MainActor
    
    The '+' encoding comment previously cited Python's parse_qsl, which
    applies to query strings, not URL fragments. The fragment is consumed
    by a JS hash.slice() with no decoding. The encoding is still defensive
    (in case an intermediate URL handler normalizes '+' to space) but the
    justification is now accurate.
    
    Also annotate @MainActor to match the isolation of webBaseURL writes
    and make the contract explicit to future callers.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * Phoenix: deep-link to web dashboard for connector management
    
    * account: add Menu and ConfirmDialog primitives for connector UX
    
    * account: full per-account connector UI in dashboard ConnectorRow
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * account: add type="button" to Connect button for consistency
    
    Matches the other three buttons added in the ConnectorRow rewrite.
    Avoids accidental form-submit if the dashboard is ever wrapped in a
    <form> element. No behavior change today.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * account: flat one-row-per-account layout for connectors
    
    Dashboard.tsx: iterate named connector keys instead of Object.values,
    which now picks up the web_base_url field I added in Task 1 and
    rendered it as a phantom third "Not connected" row.
    
    ConnectorRow.tsx: replace the ⋯ menu + nested per-account list with
    a flat one-Row-per-account layout. Each account row shows the
    connector name as title, address + (default) marker as subtitle, and
    inline [Set default] [Disconnect] action buttons. Set default hides
    on the current default row. Gmail (multi-account capable) gets a
    trailing "Add another account" row with a Connect action. Slack and
    zero-account states keep the single "Not connected + Connect" row.
    
    Menu.tsx: deleted — unused now that ConnectorRow renders inline
    buttons instead of a dropdown. Can be re-added if a future connector
    UX needs it.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * Address PR #269 review: cache activation token, soften web_base_url
    
    Two issues from the independent reviewer:
    
    1. dashboardURL() did a synchronous PhoenixSetupStore.read() (disk I/O)
       on every SwiftUI body evaluation because it's called from a
       @ViewBuilder branch in connectorSubtitle. Cache the activation
       token as a @Observable property on DaemonClient (mirroring the
       existing webBaseURL pattern), populated in checkActivationStatus
       and writeActivationToken. dashboardURL() now reads from memory.
    
    2. ConnectorsOverviewResponse.web_base_url was non-optional (str), so
       any code path that constructs the response without the field would
       throw ValidationError → HTTP 500. Phoenix already tolerates a nil
       webBaseURL via graceful-degrade to non-clickable text, so making
       the schema field Optional[str] = None is strictly safer and
       consistent with how display_name is modeled.
    
    Verified:
    - just app build → BUILD SUCCEEDED
    - just api test-fast → 535 passed, 9 subtests
    - schema test passes with the optional field
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * account: group connector rows under section headers
    
    Instead of repeating "Gmail" as the title on every row, render each
    connector as a <section> with the connector name as a small header
    and rows underneath showing only account details.
    
    Layout changes:
    - ConnectorRow returns a <section> containing an <h3> header + 1+
      rows. Single-account / disconnected case uses the same section
      structure with a single "Not connected" row.
    - Account rows now use the address as the title (with (default)
      marker as a badges slot) instead of the connector name.
    - Dashboard.tsx outer wrapper bumped from space-y-2 to space-y-6 so
      sections breathe between each other while rows inside each section
      keep the tighter space-y-2 rhythm.
    - TODO comment added on the supportsMultiAccount === "gmail"
      hardcode per the prior review's minor flag.
    
    Unrelated: also pulls in nvm-managed Node 24 LTS for local builds
    (per PR #257). The build itself is unchanged on Node 24.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * account: drop unused ConnectorStatus fields in ConnectorRow
    
    The local ConnectorStatus interface declared `account: string | null`
    and `display_name: string | null` from the pre-multi-account wire
    shape. After the rewrite, the component only reads `connected`,
    `connector`, and `accounts[]`. Trim the interface to match.
    
    The wire payload still carries those fields — TypeScript's local
    type just no longer documents them as something this component cares
    about.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * Address PR #269 review comments
    
    Three valid issues from copilot/corgea on the PR:
    
    1. api/plow/main.py: connectors_status was building the Slack response
       without populating the new `accounts` field, so the dashboard's
       `accounts.length === 0` branch rendered "Not connected" even when
       Slack was actually connected. Populate a single-entry accounts list
       for connected Slack so the wire shape is consistent across
       connectors. Phoenix wasn't affected (its subtitle falls through to
       the legacy state.accountName branch for Slack), so this only ever
       manifested in the dashboard. Caught by Copilot review; missed in
       manual verification because Slack was disconnected throughout.
    
    2. account/src/components/ConnectorRow.tsx: addAccount silently
       swallowed POST failures (no `.catch`), so a network error left the
       user staring at a non-responsive button. Add a catch that logs to
       console and surfaces a window.alert. Not pretty, but not silent.
    
    3. account/src/components/ConfirmDialog.tsx: missing dialog ARIA and
       focus management. Add role="alertdialog", aria-modal, aria-labelledby,
       aria-describedby (using useId for stable scoped IDs), and focus the
       Cancel button when the dialog opens so keyboard users land on the
       safe action by default. No focus trap (still YAGNI for a dev
       dashboard) but screen readers can now announce the dialog correctly.
    
    Skipped one comment from corgea on SettingsView.swift toggle binding —
    that's pre-existing code unrelated to this PR.
    
    Verified:
    - just api test-fast → 535 passed, 9 subtests
    - npm run build (account) → clean
    - schema test isolated → 3 passed
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

    Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit d4838cef6b763fa4d1d70c79f9eda199da3642a7
Author: swagatpatel <swagatpatel@users.noreply.github.com>

    feat: upgrade openclaw to 2026.4.12 + slim VM image (#260)
    
    * feat: upgrade openclaw from 2026.3.12 to 2026.4.12
    
    Jumps across four patch releases (4.9, 4.10, 4.11, 4.12):
    
    - 4.9: security hardening (browser SSRF re-checks after navigation,
      dotenv blocks runtime-control vars from untrusted workspaces,
      remote node exec.* sanitization, basic-ftp forced to 5.2.1 for
      CRLF CVE). Plugin-sdk command-status subpath.
    - 4.10: bundled Codex provider (Codex-managed auth/threads for
      codex/gpt-* models), Active Memory plugin, MLX local speech
      on macOS, Teams reactions/pin/read, Seedance 2.0 video gen,
      new QA lanes, openclaw exec-policy CLI command.
    - 4.11: Dreaming/memory-wiki ChatGPT import, webchat [embed] tag,
      Feishu document-thread comments, plugin manifest activation/
      setup descriptors, Ollama /api/show caching.
    - 4.12: bundled LM Studio provider, plugin activation narrowing
      (only load plugins whose manifests declare they're needed),
      Convex-backed Telegram credential leasing for QA, memory/wiki
      Unicode slug preservation, busybox/toybox removed from
      interpreter safe-bins.
    
    Dep surface grew from 51 -> 70 direct deps (+19), adding bundled
    channels for Slack, Discord/Carbon, Telegram/grammy, Feishu/Lark,
    Nostr, LanceDB, Google GenAI, OpenAI SDK (direct), jimp, mpg123,
    silk-wasm. Unpacked size 198 -> 205 MB. Node engine unchanged
    (>=22.14.0). No new bin scripts. No gatekeeper allowlist churn
    (plowd plugins.allow enumerates plugin IDs, not bundled files).
    
    Unlabeled breaking change: createTypingCallbacks was removed from
    openclaw/plugin-sdk's public exports and must now be imported from
    openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-reply-pipeline. plow-imessage's
    channel.ts has been updated to split the value import from the
    type imports. Without the fix, inbound handling silently failed
    at runtime with "createTypingCallbacks is not a function".
    
    VM pipeline: openclaw-2026.4.12.tgz (253 MB) built via
    app/scripts/build-openclaw-tgz.sh and published to
    s3://releases.plow.co/runtime/. VM image openclaw-vm-2026.4.12.tar.zst
    (390 MB) built via app/vm/build-vm-image and published to the
    same prefix. Gatekeeper not rebuilt (unchanged).
    
    Files:
    - app/vm/manifest.toml: bump openclaw_version, artifact, sha256,
      size, and openclaw_vm_* (regenerated by build-vm-image).
    - app/Phoenix/Resources/runtime-manifest.json: bump openclaw-vm
      entry only; gatekeeper-vm entry left matching S3.
    - app/plowd/plowd/gateway.py: bump PINNED_OPENCLAW_VERSION.
    - app/plowd/tests/test_gateway.py: update version assertion.
    - app/openclaw/plugins/guard/package.json: bump peerDependency.
    - app/openclaw/channels/plow-imessage/package.json + lockfile:
      bump peerDependency and regenerate lockfile.
    - app/openclaw/channels/plow-imessage/src/channel.ts: fix
      createTypingCallbacks import to new subpath.
    - app/oss-licenses.json: update openclaw version entry.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * chore: shrink openclaw VM from 409 MB to 249 MB
    
    Openclaw 4.12 bundles ~19 new channel SDKs (Slack, Discord, Telegram,
    Feishu, Nostr, LanceDB, Google GenAI, OpenAI, jimp, mpg123, silk-wasm,
    etc.) plus their transitive deps, which pushed the VM image from
    294 MB (3.12) to 409 MB. Plow doesn't use any of those channels — it
    uses its own plow-imessage bridge and plow-cli tool layer that talk
    to the plow API directly — so this commit strips everything unused
    from the VM rootfs.
    
    Savings:
    - app/vm/output/openclaw-vm-*.tar.zst: 409 MB -> 249 MB (-39%)
    - dist/extensions/: 199.5 MB -> 584 KB (kept 9 of 104 dirs)
    - openclaw/node_modules/: 942 MB -> 472 MB (stripped 29 channel deps)
    - removed pandoc apk package (235 MB binary, unused)
    - rootfs ext4 shrunk to min + 256 MB headroom (was hard-coded 2 GB)
    
    Implementation:
    - app/vm/openclaw-extensions-keep.txt: explicit keep-list for
      dist/extensions/*/. build-vm-image fails if a listed entry is
      missing upstream (drift guard for openclaw upgrades). Kept:
      anthropic, openai, active-memory, memory-core, memory-lancedb,
      memory-wiki, acpx, browser, speech-core.
    - app/vm/openclaw-node-modules-strip.txt: explicit strip-list for
      top-level dirs under openclaw/node_modules/. Silent-skip missing
      entries so the list can stay ahead of upstream churn. Strips:
      @tloncorp, @whiskeysockets, @slack, @buape, @larksuiteoapi, @line,
      @google, matrix-js-sdk, @grammyjs/grammy, @discordjs, nostr-tools,
      opusscript, silk-wasm, @azure, @mistralai, @cloudflare, @microsoft,
      @aws-sdk, @aws, @aws-crypto, @jimp/jimp, mpg123-decoder, @img,
      @opentelemetry, @esbuild/esbuild.
    - app/vm/build-vm-image: drops pandoc from apk; adds the two strip
      RUN steps after the openclaw tgz extract; replaces `resize2fs 2G`
      with `resize2fs -M` then `min + 256 MB` for minimal ext4 size.
    - speech-core and browser are kept because plow's embedded agent
      resolves `<plugin>/runtime-api.js` at runtime for both. Stripping
      speech-core produces "Unable to resolve bundled plugin public
      surface speech-core/runtime-api.js" and the agent crashes before
      reply; stripping browser breaks Camoufox CDP attach which
      plowd/gateway.py writes to openclaw.json.
    - @homebridge is NOT stripped: openclaw's core server.impl-*.js
      imports @homebridge/ciao for bonjour/mDNS advertising and would
      otherwise emit "bonjour advertising failed" at startup.
    
    Gatekeeper playwright bump (fixes browser tool under 4.12):
    - openclaw 4.12 bundles playwright-core 1.59.1, gatekeeper was
      still pinned to playwright-core 1.58.2 -- playwright enforces
      an exact client<->server version handshake, so the browser tool
      failed with "server v1.58 but all available clients are v1.59".
    - app/vm/gatekeeper/build-gatekeeper-image: bump
      playwright-core@1.58.2 -> playwright-core@1.59.1.
    - Rebuilds produce a new gatekeeper-vm-135.0.1-beta.24.tar.zst
      (camoufox itself is unchanged at 135.0.1-beta.24, only the
      playwright-core npm package that the gatekeeper installs on
      top of camoufox moves to 1.59.1).
    - Also resolves a pre-existing drift between
      app/vm/gatekeeper/manifest.toml (sha b1c5d8e4...) and what was
      actually on s3 (sha 620081b3...) that was unrelated to this PR.
    
    Tested locally with `just app build && just app up` against the
    rebuilt VMs: openclaw boots clean, guard event hooks fire, plow-cli
    tool calls succeed (gmail, imessage, read, exec), and the browser
    tool handshakes correctly with the upgraded gatekeeper.
    
    Artifacts uploaded to s3://releases.plow.co/runtime/:
    - openclaw-vm-2026.4.12.tar.zst (sha 6ba66772..., 261292384 bytes)
    - gatekeeper-vm-135.0.1-beta.24.tar.zst (sha 9e93e655..., 960038645 bytes)
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * fix: restore pandoc to VM apk install
    
    Code review flagged that the earlier slim-VM commit dropped pandoc to
    save ~53 MB, but plow's file-discovery skill at
    app/openclaw/templates/workspace/skills/plow-file-discovery/SKILL.md:59

commit 1e1f57232bc8982aed6600e691b30c335a6b5b1e
Author: Sam Odio <sam@odio.email>
Date:   Mon Apr 13 14:22:27 2026 -0700

    fix: cron PATH + file_request pipeline reliability + menubar dev icon (#252)
    
    Bundles several reliability fixes:
    
    - **A1**: VM init symlinks plow into /usr/local/bin; wrapper sets its own PYTHONPATH — cron-isolated sub-processes can now spawn('plow', ...) without ENOENT.
    - **A2**: AGENTS.md 'Never claim a tool is unavailable' rule — agent must call the tool and quote error.detail instead of hallucinating 'tool not supported'.
    - **B1**: file_search shell-parses mdfind queries via shlex, supports -onlyin with ~ expansion, drops unknown flags, adds structured argv/rc/lines logging.
    - **B2**: file-request pipeline routes every failure branch through _fail_file_request; try/finally watchdog on _process_file_request catches silent-drops and CancelledError; strong task refs prevent GC mid-flight; new awaiting_approval state avoids watchdog false-positives on legitimate handoffs.
    - **B3**: plow_file_request tool description and plow-file-discovery skill rewritten to lock down the sync 'requested' vs async [system] follow-up contract so the agent stops inventing UX narration.
    - **Followups**: user-ready 503 detail, mkdir -p /usr/local/bin defensive guard, menubar dev icon tint via sourceIn compositing + colorScheme reactivity, /plow-activate doc polish, WS keepalive comment.
    
    Live-verified end-to-end on wt3 against slowdown: fresh VM rebuild + rmup + activate → agent intro in 18s → plow_gmail_status round-trip in 21s with no ENOENT → plow_file_request round-trip finding a real file on Desktop in 15s.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

    * Address remaining Gmail restoration review feedback
    
    Fixes the substantive issues flagged on PR #201 that were deferred in
    17970986, plus the cosmetic items from the follow-up review round.
    
    Correctness:
    - Reply-all now parses To/Cc via email.utils.getaddresses instead of
      str.split(","), so quoted display names like "Last, First" <a@b> no
      longer get torn at the comma. Self-exclusion compares RFC 5322
      addr-specs instead of doing a substring match, which previously would
      have dropped other-me@example.com when self was me@example.com.
      Primary recipient is also deduped against the extras.
    - Reply now chains the References header per RFC 5322
      (parent References + parent Message-Id) rather than replacing the
      chain with just the parent Message-Id. Fixes broken threading in
      non-Gmail MUAs that rebuild threads from headers.
    
    Cleanup:
    - Merge duplicate .schemas import and drop the GmailApiDraftRefSchema
      alias (no actual name collision).
    - Finish the default_factory sweep for _GmailApiPayload.body.
    - Drop the always-True `deleted` field from GmailDeleteDraftResult.
    - Collapse the four archive/star/read/unread parser declarations into
      a loop driven by _LABEL_SHORTCUTS, which now carries help text too.
    - Forward subject prefix check also matches "fw:" in addition to "fwd:".
    
    Tests (+10 in test_connector.py):
    - _build_reply_to_list: primary preserved verbatim, quoted display
      names with commas survive parsing, self excluded by addr-spec not
      substring, primary deduped against extras, no extras when not
      reply-all.
    - reply_gmail_message: References chains existing chain + Message-Id,
      falls back to just Message-Id on first reply, threads via threadId
      and prefixes subject with Re:.
    - forward_gmail_message: binary attachment bytes survive the
      base64url decode/re-encode round trip through the outbound MIME.
    - _build_mime_attachment: malformed MIME type fails loudly.

    * fix: isolate `just app test` and `just app rmup` by worktree variant
    
    Two related fixes so running the app recipes in one worktree stops
    tripping over state belonging to other worktrees.
    
    - `just app test` now reads PLOW_VARIANT from .env.local and passes the
      variant-scoped PLOW_APP_NAME and PLOW_APP_BUNDLE_IDENTIFIER to
      xcodebuild. Previously it hardcoded "Plow Dev" / co.plow.app.dev, so
      unit tests that exercised AppPaths scribbled into the non-variant
      Application Support dir regardless of which worktree ran them.
    
    - `just app rmup` resolves the variant the same way and scopes the
      pgrep kill, osascript quit, and rm -rf to the exact effective bundle
      id / app name. The previous glob (co.plow.app.dev*) would wipe every
      sibling worktree's Application Support dir, and the substring pgrep
      would kill any other running variant's processes.

    * fix: drop unmapped claude-sonnet-4-6-20250627 from ALLOWED_MODELS
    
    litellm doesn't have model_info for `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6-20250627`,
    so `litellm.get_model_info()` raises "This model isn't mapped yet" when
    GET /v1/models iterates ALLOWED_MODELS. That bubbles up as a 500, which
    breaks plowd startup: `plowd/main.py:225` calls `fetch_model_list()` in
    `_provision_gateway_credentials` and retries 6× before failing, and the
    Phoenix app retries plowd 3× before surfacing
    "Error: PlowD failed to start after 3 attempts" in the installer.
    
    The dated ID is redundant — `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6` already
    resolves to Sonnet 4.6 with a 1M context window in litellm, and nothing
    else in the repo references the dated variant.

    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * plow-activate: replace tail+grep+head monitor with polling loop
    
    The Step 5 activation-code extraction was using
    `tail -F | grep --line-buffered | head -3` wrapped in a Monitor task,
    and silently missed two back-to-back codes (OK3RW, then IM19S) in a
    single session. Diagnosed root causes:
    
    1. head -3 exits as soon as three lines match and tears down the
       upstream pipe before the Monitor framework flushes stdout. Combined
       with grep's residual buffering, a single match can be produced,
       consumed by head, and the whole pipe torn down before any event
       reaches the watcher.
    2. rmup deletes the Application Support dir, so app.log's inode
       changes. tail -F polls by name but has a ~1s window where lines
       written via Swift's appendAppLog (which opens a fresh FileHandle
       per call) can land on the new inode before tail reopens it.
    3. If a first activation code times out and the installer retries, a
       second `Activation created:` line appears in the same log. head -3
       from the top might capture the stale one.
    
    Replace the pipe with a polling loop: `grep | tail -1` re-opens the
    file each tick, always picks the freshest match, and has no pipe
    state to lose. This is the Option A fix documented in the post-mortem;
    Option B (Swift writes a structured pending-activation.json) remains a
    follow-up.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * refactor(gmail): route all URL lookups through settings, drop parallel constants
    
    Root cause of the test-scenarios Gmail failures: the connector module had
    two parallel sources of truth for Google/Gmail URLs — module-level
    constants in api/plow/config.py AND matching fields on the Settings
    class. The connector's kwarg-threading scheme was half-implemented: 4
    of the 13 public functions accepted a `token_url=` / `api_base_url=`
    kwarg defaulted to the module constant, but the other 9 hardcoded
    GMAIL_API_BASE_URL directly in their bodies. Even for the 4 functions
    with kwargs, the router at api/plow/connectors/gmail/router.py never
    passed them, so every data-path call hit real Google regardless of the
    compose.test.yaml twin URL override. In tests this surfaced as
    "Google OAuth token refresh HTTP 401: invalid_client" against the
    dtu-google-oauth twin expecting a different client.
    
    This refactor deletes the parallel track:
    
    - api/plow/config.py: drop GOOGLE_OAUTH_BASE_URL, GOOGLE_OAUTH_TOKEN_URL,
      GMAIL_API_BASE_URL module constants. Inline their default strings on
      the corresponding Settings fields. Settings is now the only source of
      truth for these URLs.
    
    - api/plow/connectors/gmail/connector.py: import `settings` directly
      instead of the three dead constants. Delete the unused
      GOOGLE_OAUTH_AUTHORIZE_URL module constant. Drop the
      `oauth_base_url=` / `token_url=` / `api_base_url=` kwargs from
      build_gmail_oauth_url, exchange_gmail_authorization_code,
      refresh_gmail_access_token, fetch_gmail_profile, list_gmail_messages,
      get_gmail_message, and send_gmail_message — 11 kwarg lines removed.
      Every URL reference in every function body now reads
      `settings.google_oauth_base_url`, `settings.google_oauth_token_url`,
      or `settings.gmail_api_base_url` directly, including the 9 body sites
      (reply, forward, get_attachment, labels, modify_labels, list_drafts,
      create_draft, send_draft, delete_draft) that were hardcoded to
      GMAIL_API_BASE_URL and therefore impossible to override by any
      caller. Matches the existing settings-import pattern in
      provider.py and router.py.
    
    - api/plow/connectors/gmail/provider.py: drop the redundant
      `oauth_base_url=` / `token_url=` / `api_base_url=` kwargs from the
      build_gmail_oauth_url, exchange_gmail_authorization_code, and
      fetch_gmail_profile call sites. Remove the now-unused
      `from plow.config import settings` import.
    
    - api/schemas/plow_schemas/api/gmail.py: relax GmailGetRequest.account
      from `str` to `str | None = None` so read ops can default to the
      user's default Gmail account, matching GmailListRequest and
      GmailSendRequest. The server-side _require_gmail_connection in
      router.py already treats account_identifier=None as "use the default
      account", this change lets clients actually take advantage of that.
    
    - api/plow/connectors/gmail/router.py: when annotating the response for
      messages.get, fall back to `connection.email_address` if the request
      didn't specify an account, so the returned GmailFullMessage carries
      the actual account identifier instead of None.
    
    - cli/plow/commands/gmail.py: drop the client-side `_require_account`
      gate from the `get` subcommand. The server already handles None by
      defaulting to the default account, and this matches how `search` and
      `send` already work. Reply, forward, and the label/draft mutations
      still require --account explicitly because they modify state.
    
    - app/justfile: drop `-quiet` from the `test` recipe so future xctest
      case failures show the failing Test Case name inline instead of
      getting swallowed into the xcresult bundle in DerivedData.
    
    Net LOC: ~-20. The bug is fully fixed — every gmail API call now honors
    the twin URL override in dev/test environments — and there's one source
    of truth per URL instead of the previous constant + field pair.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * fix: persist env-derived URLs before macOS can strip --env on restart
    
    plow-activate kept losing the VM API URL after macOS relaunched the app
    for a Full Disk Access grant. The chain:
    
    1. `just app rmup` launches Plow Dev wt3 via `open -W --env
       PLOW_VM_PLOW_API_URL=https://plow.ngrok.io ... Plow Dev wt3.app`.
    2. The first process has the env var set, but nothing calls
       `ServiceURLs.vmPlowAPIURL()` on that process — the only callers live
       in `DaemonClient.daemonLaunchConfig`, which doesn't fire until after
       activation succeeds.
    3. macOS grants Full Disk Access and restarts the app via
       LSOpenCFURLRef. The restarted process has zero PLOW_ env vars.
    4. Activation eventually completes, `daemonLaunchConfig` runs, and
       `vmPlowAPIURL()` is called for the first time — on the *second*
       process, with no env var, so it falls through to
       `cloudAPIURL()` and plowd writes `http://slowdown:8000` into the
       VM's `openclaw/gateway/plow-api-url`. The OpenClaw plow-ws client
       then fails to reach the API and the agent never greets.
    
    The four getters that VMManager happens to call in its init
    (`plowdPort`, `gatewayPort`, `vmShellPort`, `cloudAPIURL`) don't hit
    this bug because their persistence side effect runs on the first
    process, before the restart. `vmPlowAPIURL` was the odd one out:
    nothing read it early, so its persistence path was dead on first
    launch.
    
    Fix: add an explicit `ServiceURLs.captureEnvironmentForRestartSurvival`
    that reads every env-derived URL/port once so each getter persists to
    disk, and call it from `PhoenixApp.init` before anything else runs.
    This consolidates the "capture env vars early" concern in one place
    with documentation, instead of relying on the implicit coincidence
    that VMManager's init happens to read most of them.
    
    Ran `just app test` → TEST SUCCEEDED.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
