commit 4f0b3f8b2b030f38de7d8bfd29b28f407951a465
Author: plonkus <plucas@gmail.com>
Date:   Wed Apr 1 10:39:02 2026 -0700

    Fix VM runtime check blocking fresh installs (#121)
    
    * Fix daemon launch checking for VM images in app bundle instead of download cache
    
    VM images (vmlinuz, rootfs.img) are now downloaded to runtime-cache/ by
    RuntimeDownloadManager, but missingRuntimePaths still looked for them in
    Bundle.main.resourceURL. This caused "runtime incomplete" errors on every
    fresh install. Remove the stale checks — VM readiness is already gated by
    RuntimeDownloadManager.openclawVMReady / vmBundlePath().
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * Fix app icon by disabling asset catalog icon generation
    
    Xcode's asset catalog compiler generates a default app icon when
    ASSETCATALOG_COMPILER_APPICON_NAME is unset, overriding the pre-built
    AppIcon.icns referenced by CFBundleIconFile. Set the build setting to
    empty so the .icns is used directly.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    ---------
    
    Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

    design assets. Add DocumentIcon, FolderIcon, and AboutLogo to the
    asset catalog. Apply the custom folder icon to ~/Plow at app startup
    and after folder creation using NSWorkspace.setIcon.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * Resize app icon for HIG compliance and composite folder icon at runtime
    
    Scale AppIcon artwork to 75% on transparent canvas so it matches the
    standard macOS icon proportions in Launchpad/Dock. Replace static
    FolderIcon.iconset with runtime compositing — overlays the Plow logo
    badge onto the live system folder icon via NSWorkspace, so the icon
    automatically matches the current macOS folder style across OS versions.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * Restore full-bleed app icon and use monochrome folder badge
    
    Revert AppIcon PNGs to original full-bleed design exports — macOS 11+
    auto-applies the squircle mask so all apps render at the same size.
    Replace the color PlowBadge with a white template glyph (letterform
    only, no background) and tint it to folder-blue at runtime, matching

    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * Fix app icon size (80% per Apple template) and folder badge style
    
    Scale AppIcon to 80% on transparent canvas — 819px content in 1024px,
    matching Apple's ~824px template spec. Removes rounded rect border
    from PlowBadge (letterform only). Adjusts folder badge vertical
    position to 56% from top to center on the folder face.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * Use pre-built AppIcon.icns to preserve icon margins
    
    Xcode's asset catalog compiler strips transparent padding from icon
    PNGs, making the icon appear larger than peers. Switch to a pre-built
    .icns file via CFBundleIconFile, bypassing the asset catalog. The
    built app now preserves the 80% content area (~102px margin at 1024px),
    matching the standard icon proportions.
    
    Also fixes folder badge: removes rounded rect border from template,
    adjusts vertical position to 56% from top.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * Remove AppIcon.appiconset — asset catalog was overriding pre-built .icns
    
    Xcode's asset catalog compiler regenerates AppIcon.icns from the
    appiconset, stripping transparent margins. Remove the appiconset
    entirely so the pre-built AppIcon.icns (with 80% fill / 102px margins)
    is used as-is in the bundle.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

    Use .sourceAtop blend mode so green tint only applies to opaque folder
    pixels, not the transparent background. Request 1024x1024 system
    folder icon for sharp rendering at all sizes.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * Dispatch PlowFolderIcon to main thread from DaemonClient
    
    AppKit drawing (NSImage, lockFocus, NSWorkspace.setIcon) must run on
    the main thread. DaemonClient.ensureDaemonRunningIfNeeded() may not
    be on @MainActor, so dispatch the call async to main.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * Use hue blend mode for folder tint to preserve 3D shading
    
    CGBlendMode.hue changes the folder's color from blue to green while
    preserving luminance and saturation — keeping highlights, shadows,
    tab outline, and depth visible. A destinationIn pass restores the
    original alpha mask.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * Use CIHueAdjust for folder tint — preserves shading and alpha
    
    Replace broken blend mode approach with CIHueAdjust filter that
    rotates hue by -1.2 radians (blue to green). Preserves all 3D

commit 9f4ddb27dc53441c0c750a36788ba8ce53761897
Author: Daniel Delattre <55804946+delattre1@users.noreply.github.com>
Date:   Mon Mar 30 15:39:21 2026 -0700

    Fix crash when clicking Settings after closing app window (#89)
    
    * Fix settings crash: nil out window ref in windowWillClose
    
    NSWindow created via init(contentRect:styleMask:backing:defer:) defaults
    to isReleasedWhenClosed=true. After close, AppKit releases the window
    behind ARC's back, leaving self.window as a dangling pointer. The next
    show() call dereferences the zombie on `window.isVisible`, causing
    EXC_BAD_ACCESS. Nil out self.window in windowWillClose to prevent this.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * Prevent app quit when closing window; keep menu bar alive
    
    SwiftUI's default lifecycle terminates the app when the last window
    closes. Add NSApplicationDelegateAdaptor returning false from
    applicationShouldTerminateAfterLastWindowClosed so the app stays
    running as a menu bar extra. Cmd+Q still quits normally.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * Hide window on close instead of destroying it; keep menu bar alive
    
    windowShouldClose now calls dismiss() (orderOut + accessory policy)
    and returns false, preventing the actual NSWindow close. This avoids
    both the zombie reference crash and the SwiftUI lifecycle termination
    that occurred when the last window was destroyed.
    
    show() now checks `if let window` (not `window.isVisible`) to re-show
    the hidden window instead of creating a new one each time.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * Merge window-close plans into one file
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * Remove dead AppDelegate and window=nil code (YAGNI)
    
    windowShouldClose returns false, so the window never actually closes.
    That means windowWillClose never fires (window=nil is dead code) and
    applicationShouldTerminateAfterLastWindowClosed is never consulted
    (AppDelegate is dead code). Remove both.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    ---------
    
    Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 1 file changed, 7 insertions(+), 4 deletions(-)

commit 9a7590373e3ba40e3c9c8760e0c25e2ae7e9feea
Author: Sam Odio <sam@odio.email>
Date:   Mon Mar 30 10:45:14 2026 -0700

    Gatekeeper VM split: parallel VMs with S3-downloaded images (#63)
    
    Split the single Alpine VM into two parallel VMs — OpenClaw (Alpine) for the
    gateway and Gatekeeper (Debian) for Camoufox browser automation. VM images
    are no longer bundled in the app; they are downloaded from S3 on first launch.
    
    Architecture:
    - OpenClaw VM (Alpine, 2GB): runs the OpenClaw gateway with baked-in binary
    - Gatekeeper VM (Debian, 4GB, 2 GiB RAM): runs Camoufox via Playwright with
      baked-in browser binary, communicates with OpenClaw via cross-VM vsock bridge
    - Both VMs boot from tar.zst images downloaded from S3 (~280MB + ~915MB)
    - App bundle no longer ships VM images — much smaller install size
    - Local dev bypasses S3 via PLOW_VM_BUNDLE / PLOW_GATEKEEPER_VM_BUNDLE env vars
    
    Build pipeline:
    - build-vm-image bakes OpenClaw tgz into Alpine rootfs
    - build-gatekeeper-image bakes Camoufox zip into Debian rootfs (preserving
      chmod +x for zip archives that strip Unix permissions)
    - Both scripts produce tar.zst tarballs and write SHA256/size to manifest.toml
    - generate-runtime-manifest.py reads both manifests to produce runtime-manifest.json
    
    Swift app:
    - RuntimeDownloadManager downloads VM tarballs, extracts via /usr/bin/tar,
      sets verified flags eagerly on background queue (no SHA256 on main thread)
    - VMManager and GatekeeperVMManager accept vmBundle URL parameter
    - DaemonClient gates OpenClaw VM on openclawVMReady, Gatekeeper on
      gatekeeperVMReady + OpenClaw VM running (prevents startup ordering bugs)
    - Installer advances past download screen once OpenClaw VM is ready;
      Gatekeeper downloads in background
    - Rootfs copy verified immediately after copyItem with retry on truncation
      (works around APFS deferred allocation producing partial copies)
    - Cross-VM CDP bridge uses bidirectionalRelayFDs with proper fd lifecycle
      (closeOnDealloc:false, single teardown closure, no double-close)
    - Polls for Camoufox WS endpoint file instead of hard-coded 20s sleep
    
    VM init scripts:
    - Removed runtime-cache virtiofs mount and artifact extraction from both VMs
    - OpenClaw and Camoufox binaries are pre-installed in the rootfs
    - Preserved DHCP networking fix (sleep + verbose dhclient) in Gatekeeper VM
    
    Packaging:
    - plowd-package-runtime no longer copies VM images into app bundle
    - plowd-validate-package checks runtime-manifest.json entries instead
    - justfile sets VM bundle env vars in dev mode, runtime recipe builds and
      deploys VM tarballs to S3
    
    Other:
    - Renamed camoufox_cdp_url → camoufox_ws_url (it's a Playwright WS endpoint)
    - GatekeeperVMManager.stop() simplified (removed dead isRunning check)
    - Gatekeeper VM memory bumped from 1 GiB to 2 GiB for Camoufox headroom

commit 41cb393415cdf0f0c1871c9f8be92e6f1007541d
Author: plonkus <plucas@gmail.com>
Date:   Thu Mar 26 15:12:46 2026 -0700

    Lock Phoenix signing to CNCorp Apple team (#76)
    
    * Lock app signing to cncorp Apple team
    
    * Resume runtime install after completed setup
    
    * Clarify release signing fallback docs
    
    * Decouple runtime artifacts from release pipeline
    
    The release script was rebuilding and re-uploading runtime artifacts to
    S3, which could silently overwrite them with different content and break
    the manifest hash. Runtime artifacts should be managed independently via
    `just runtime` with their own PR cycle.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * Regenerate bundled runtime manifest and update tests
    
    The bundled runtime-manifest.json was stale after the openclaw hash
    changed in manifest.toml. Also flip the release script test to assert
    runtime uploads are absent, matching the decoupling in the prior commit.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * Remove signing fallback and stop auto-regenerating runtime manifest
    
    - Remove PLOW_CODESIGN_FALLBACK_TO_ANY_TEAM: the release script now
      requires the exact team identity with no fallback path.
    - Stop regenerating runtime-manifest.json on every build. The manifest
      is now only updated explicitly via `just runtime`, with a test guard
      that catches drift between manifest.toml and the bundled JSON.
    - Update docs and tests to match.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * Fix stale AppPaths test assertions
    
    PLOW_LAUNCH_MODE and PLOW_REPO_ROOT moved out of AppPaths.swift earlier
    on this branch. The test was checking for their presence but the
    important guard (runtimeCacheDirectory uses bundled paths, not env
    overrides) is still covered.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * Clarify that release rebuilds local runtime but does not publish it
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * Regenerate runtime manifest after deploy
    
    `just runtime deploy` now auto-regenerates runtime-manifest.json after
    uploading to S3, so the committed manifest always reflects what's
    actually deployed.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * Fix S3 key format in release docs
    
    The docs showed flat keys (Plow.dmg) but the script uploads versioned
    keys (downloads/Plow-<version>-<build>.dmg).
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * Hardcode production S3 bucket/prefix for runtime artifacts
    
    Remove configurable s3_bucket/s3_prefix from `just runtime` to avoid
    accidentally writing staging URLs into the committed manifest. The
    production bucket (releases.plow.co/runtime) is the only supported
    target. Also remove dead PLOW_RUNTIME_RELEASES_* env exports from the
    release recipe.

commit b595d05b7c08a56b7c0e0d4b2e0ed4269207b548
Author: Sam Odio <sam@odio.email>
Date:   Wed Mar 25 14:09:49 2026 -0700

    Fix PlowD never starting on dev relaunch (#74)
    
    runtimeCacheDirectory() had a dev-mode override that redirected the
    cache path to $PLOW_REPO_ROOT/.build/runtime-cache/ when launched with
    PLOW_LAUNCH_MODE=dev. The initial install (without those env vars) wrote
    artifacts and .manifest-hash to ~/Library/Application Support/..., so
    dev relaunches via `just up` looked in the wrong directory, never called
    startDownloads(), and downloadManager.status stayed .idle forever —
    blocking PlowD from launching.
    
    Remove the dev-mode path override. The bundle ID already separates dev
    from prod (co.plow.app.dev vs co.plow.app), consistent with every other
    path in AppPaths.
    
    Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 1 file changed, 3 insertions(+), 14 deletions(-)

commit d738710cbe2a87f228ba7ff0a20774ee7b9ee530
Author: Daniel Delattre <55804946+delattre1@users.noreply.github.com>
Date:   Tue Mar 24 09:59:30 2026 -0700

    Suppress "Could not connect" error during initial launch/VM boot (#64)
    
    During fresh install, the status poll fires before PlowD is reachable
    (VM is still copying rootfs and booting). Previously this set the error
    to "Could not connect to the server" which was misleading — the daemon
    just hasn't started yet. Now we suppress the UI error when the daemon
    process isn't running or the app is still launching, and log it as an
    expected transient state instead.
    
    Co-authored-by: daniel <delattre@daniels-MacBook-Pro.local>
    Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

    * refactor: remove slot math from Swift, launcher is single port source
    
    PortSlot.swift now reads explicit env vars (PLOW_PLOWD_PORT,
    PLOW_GATEWAY_PORT, PLOW_API_PORT) set by the launcher. No hash
    function, no CommonCrypto import, no slot computation in Swift.
    
    - app/justfile passes explicit port env vars to the app process
    - Stops passing PLOW_PORT_SLOT (app no longer needs it)
    - Removes portSlotDescription from diagnostics

    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * refactor: URL-based service config, split host vs VM API URLs
    
    Runtime contract is now URL-based, not port-based:
    - Launcher passes PLOW_PLOWD_URL, PLOW_GATEWAY_URL, PLOW_API_URL
    - Swift reads full URLs via ServiceURLs (renamed from PortSlot)
    - VMManager parses ports from URLs at the edge for bridge setup
    
    Explicit host/VM API URL split:
    - PLOW_API_URL: host-side API (http://127.0.0.1:{slot-derived port})
    - PLOW_OPENCLAW_API_URL: VM-facing API (http://127.0.0.1:8000, the
      guest-local bridged endpoint via Bridge E)
    - DaemonClient.resolveOpenClawAPIURL() passes the VM-facing URL to
      plowd, which writes it to /config/gateway/plow-api-url
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * refactor: ports for plowd/gateway, URLs for API, split host vs VM API
    
    Config contract:
    - PLOW_PLOWD_PORT, PLOW_GATEWAY_PORT: ports (always loopback)
    - PLOW_API_URL: host-side API URL (app + plowd host fetches)
    - PLOW_VM_API_URL: VM-facing API URL (guest-local bridged endpoint)
    
    plowd receives both:
    - PLOWD_PLOW_API_URL: host-side, for manifest/model fetches
    - PLOWD_VM_API_URL: written to /config/gateway/plow-api-url for VM
    
    Fixes the bug where dev plowd was getting the VM-facing URL
    (127.0.0.1:8000) for its own host-side fetches instead of the
    compose API port.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

    plow_api_url instead of the VM-facing vm_api_url:

    Added resolved_vm_api_url property on GatewayConfig that falls
    back to plow_api_url when vm_api_url is not set (correct for prod).

    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * refactor: collapse PLOWD_PLOW_API_URL/PLOWD_VM_PLOW_API_URL into shared PLOW_*
    
    Shared runtime env vars use PLOW_* prefix (not PLOWD_*):
    - PLOW_API_URL: host-side API URL (read by app + plowd)
    - PLOW_VM_PLOW_API_URL: VM-facing API URL (read by plowd for VM config)
    
    PLOWD_* prefix reserved for daemon-private config only:
    - PLOWD_APP_SUPPORT_ROOT, PLOWD_OPENCLAW_*, etc.
    
    plowd config.py uses pydantic validation_alias to read PLOW_API_URL
    and PLOW_VM_PLOW_API_URL directly (bypassing PLOWD_ prefix).
    
    DaemonClient forwards the shared PLOW_* names to plowd instead of
    minting PLOWD_ copies. Docs updated to match.

    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    
    * refactor: separate backend and app lifecycles
    
    Backend (compose) and app are now independent:
    - `just api up`: starts full compose stack (db + api + dtu) in
      foreground. Logs visible. Ctrl-C stops it. Run in its own tab.
    - `just app up`: builds and launches macOS app only. No compose
      lifecycle. Kill and relaunch freely without restarting backend.
    - `just api down`: tears down compose stack.
    
    Compose runs in foreground (not -d) so API/DTU logs are visible
    in the terminal. The app cleanup no longer tears down compose.

codex
### Investigation of Finding 1

**Calibration answers:**

**Q1: Will users at this early-product operating point hit inconsistent behavior here, or is the only evidence potential future copy drift with no observed instances?**
A: Users can hit two presentations of the same control, but the current code does not show inconsistent behavior: both surfaces bind to `$keepMacAwake.isEnabled` and use the same switch/tint at `app/Phoenix/SettingsView.swift:198` and `app/Phoenix/StatusView.swift:437`. The copy/chrome is intentionally different today: Settings has icon + subtitle at `app/Phoenix/SettingsView.swift:185` and `app/Phoenix/SettingsView.swift:191`, while Status has only a compact `HStack` label at `app/Phoenix/StatusView.swift:433`. The claimed tooltip drift is not present: tooltip/help grep finds Settings tooltips elsewhere and only `StatusView`’s Tools help at `app/Phoenix/StatusView.swift:414`, not a Keep Mac Awake tooltip. Firing-rate evidence for an actual user-visible inconsistency is therefore zero observed instances; the evidence is future drift risk. Confidence: high.

**Q2: Could this be handled by deleting one duplicate surface or aligning copy inline, instead of adding a shared view component?**
A: Yes. The simpler contract is “one shared `KeepMacAwake` model, local UI chrome per surface.” That is already what the code does: `PhoenixApp` owns one model at `app/Phoenix/PhoenixApp.swift:42`, Settings receives it at `app/Phoenix/SettingsView.swift:6`, and Status receives it at `app/Phoenix/StatusView.swift:177`. Deleting one surface would change the inferred product intent, which explicitly says the PR adds a persistent toggle in both `SettingsView.swift` and `StatusView.swift` (`.codex-scratch/inferred-intent.md:1`). If exact copy matters, inline alignment is smaller than a shared component: add/remove the one subtitle line cluster around `app/Phoenix/SettingsView.swift:191`, or add matching copy near `app/Phoenix/StatusView.swift:434`. Confidence: high.

**Pattern search:**
- Existing Phoenix UI patterns favor local helpers inside the owning surface: `SettingsView` keeps `settingsRow` local at `app/Phoenix/SettingsView.swift:416`, and `StatusView` keeps `toolsMenu` and `statusHeaderView` local at `app/Phoenix/StatusView.swift:326` and `app/Phoenix/StatusView.swift:418`.
- `git grep -n "Keep Mac Awake" -- app app/PhoenixTests` returns only the two UI labels at `app/Phoenix/SettingsView.swift:188` and `app/Phoenix/StatusView.swift:434`; there are no tests documenting a requirement that the two rows remain text-identical.
- Related history shows the two surfaces were added separately: commit `e0b51774347e324a735abe4f322ee9cd803fe0cb` added the Settings System section, and commit `48419b4b1a2ce3a375b84570c38e8da9729b9611` added the Status panel toggle. The existing extraction precedent is intra-file reuse, not cross-surface components.
- Original remedy is ~25 LOC across 2 files; inline copy alignment would be ~1-4 LOC, and leaving local chrome unchanged is 0 LOC.

**Decline-history check:**
- No prior decline. `.codex-scratch/decline-history.md` has no non-empty lines.

**Recommendation:** REFRAME
- The duplicate is real, but under the early-product operating point it is not broken glass users are currently cutting themselves on. The current shared contract is the important one: both rows drive the same observable model. A cross-file shared component would make the UI harder to iterate while the product is still discovering where this toggle belongs.
- Will both Keep Mac Awake surfaces stay intentionally identical in copy and affordance? If yes, align the copy inline first and extract only after observed drift or a third surface appears. If not, consider cutting the shared component request — it adds complexity and makes PMF iteration harder.
